     631             : 
     632             : /**
     633             :  * \brief Constructor.
     634             :  *
     635             :  * This constructor takes an optional string argument which if passed
     636             :  * should be a WKT representation of an SRS.  Passing this is equivalent
     637             :  * to not passing it, and then calling importFromWkt() with the WKT string.
     638             :  *
     639             :  * Note that newly created objects are given a reference count of one.
     640             :  *
     641             :  * The C function OSRNewSpatialReference() does the same thing as this
     642             :  * constructor.
     643             :  *
     644             :  * @param pszWKT well known text definition to which the object should
     645             :  * be initialized, or NULL (the default).
     646             :  */
     647             : 
     648       77789 : OGRSpatialReference::OGRSpatialReference( const char * pszWKT ) :
     649       77789 :     d(new Private())
     650             : {
     651       77807 :     if( pszWKT != nullptr )
     652        1009 :         importFromWkt( pszWKT );
     653       77807 : }

     715             : 
     716             : /**

     719             :  * This static method will destroy a OGRSpatialReference.  It is
     720             :  * equivalent to calling delete on the object, but it ensures that the
     721             :  * deallocation is properly executed within the OGR libraries heap on
     722             :  * platforms where this can matter (win32).

     724             :  * This function is the same as OSRDestroySpatialReference()

     972             : /************************************************************************/
     973             : /*                            GetAttrNode()                             */
     974             : /************************************************************************/
     975             : 
     976             : /**
     977             :  * \brief Find named node in tree.
     978             :  *
     979             :  * This method does a pre-order traversal of the node tree searching for
     980             :  * a node with this exact value (case insensitive), and returns it.  Leaf
     981             :  * nodes are not considered, under the assumption that they are just
     982             :  * attribute value nodes.
     983             :  *
     984             :  * If a node appears more than once in the tree (such as UNIT for instance),
     985             :  * the first encountered will be returned.  Use GetNode() on a subtree to be
     986             :  * more specific.
     987             :  *
     988             :  * @param pszNodePath the name of the node to search for.  May contain multiple
     989             :  * components such as "GEOGCS|UNIT".
     990             :  *
     991             :  * @return a pointer to the node found, or NULL if none.
     992             :  */
     993             : 
     994       49270 : OGR_SRSNode *OGRSpatialReference::GetAttrNode( const char * pszNodePath )
     995             : 
     996             : {
     997       49270 :     if( strchr(pszNodePath, '|') == nullptr )
     998             :     {
     999             :         // Fast path
    1000       31132 :         OGR_SRSNode *poNode = GetRoot();
    1001       31132 :         if( poNode )
    1002       30945 :             poNode = poNode->GetNode( pszNodePath );
    1003       31132 :         return poNode;
    1004             :     }
    1005             : 
    1006             :     char **papszPathTokens =
    1007       18138 :         CSLTokenizeStringComplex(pszNodePath, "|", TRUE, FALSE);
    1008             : 
    1009       18138 :     if( CSLCount( papszPathTokens ) < 1 )
    1010             :     {
    1011           0 :         CSLDestroy(papszPathTokens);
    1012           0 :         return nullptr;
    1013             :     }
    1014             : 
    1015       18138 :     OGR_SRSNode *poNode = GetRoot();
    1016       56857 :     for( int i = 0; poNode != nullptr && papszPathTokens[i] != nullptr; i++ )
    1017             :     {
    1018       38719 :         poNode = poNode->GetNode( papszPathTokens[i] );
    1019             :     }
    1020             : 
    1021       18138 :     CSLDestroy( papszPathTokens );
    1022             : 
    1023       18138 :     return poNode;
    1024             : }
    1025             : 
    1026             : /**
    1027             :  * \brief Find named node in tree.
    1028             :  *
    1029             :  * This method does a pre-order traversal of the node tree searching for
    1030             :  * a node with this exact value (case insensitive), and returns it.  Leaf
    1031             :  * nodes are not considered, under the assumption that they are just
    1032             :  * attribute value nodes.
    1033             :  *
    1034             :  * If a node appears more than once in the tree (such as UNIT for instance),
    1035             :  * the first encountered will be returned.  Use GetNode() on a subtree to be
    1036             :  * more specific.
    1037             :  *
    1038             :  * @param pszNodePath the name of the node to search for.  May contain multiple
    1039             :  * components such as "GEOGCS|UNIT".

    1042             :  */
    1043             : 
    1044             : const OGR_SRSNode *
    1045       39458 : OGRSpatialReference::GetAttrNode( const char * pszNodePath ) const
    1046             :

    1054             : /************************************************************************/
    1055             : /*                            GetAttrValue()                            */
    1056             : /************************************************************************/
    1057             : 
    1058             : /**
    1059             :  * \brief Fetch indicated attribute of named node.
    1060             :  *
    1061             :  * This method uses GetAttrNode() to find the named node, and then extracts
    1062             :  * the value of the indicated child.  Thus a call to GetAttrValue("UNIT",1)
    1063             :  * would return the second child of the UNIT node, which is normally the
    1064             :  * length of the linear unit in meters.
    1065             :  *
    1066             :  * This method does the same thing as the C function OSRGetAttrValue().
    1067             :  *
    1068             :  * @param pszNodeName the tree node to look for (case insensitive).
    1069             :  * @param iAttr the child of the node to fetch (zero based).
    1070             :  *
    1071             :  * @return the requested value, or NULL if it fails for any reason.
    1072             :  */

    1218             : 
    1219             : /**
    1220             :  * Convert this SRS into a nicely formatted WKT 1 string for display to a person.

    1223             :  * for implementation details of WKT 1 in OGR.

    1226             :  * CPLFree() when no longer needed.  It is the responsibility of the caller.

    1229             :  * option. Valid values are the one of the FORMAT option of
    1230             :  * exportToWkt( char ** ppszResult, const char* const* papszOptions ) const

    1232             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRExportToPrettyWkt().
    1233             :  *
    1234             :  * @param ppszResult the resulting string is returned in this pointer.
    1235             :  * @param bSimplify TRUE if the AXIS, AUTHORITY and EXTENSION nodes should be
    1236             :  *   stripped off.
    1237             :  *
    1238             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE if successful.
    1239             :  */

    1249           0 :         aosOptions.SetNameValue("FORMAT", "WKT1_SIMPLE");
    1250             :     }
    1251          69 :     return exportToWkt( ppszResult, aosOptions.List() );
    1252             : }

    1311             : /************************************************************************/

    1314             : 
    1315             : /**
    1316             :  * Convert this SRS into a WKT string.
    1317             :  *
    1318             :  * Note that the returned WKT string should be freed with
    1319             :  * CPLFree() when no longer needed.  It is the responsibility of the caller.
    1320             :  *
    1321             :  * Consult also the <a href="wktproblems.html">OGC WKT Coordinate System Issues</a> page
    1322             :  * for implementation details of WKT 1 in OGR.
    1323             :  *
    1324             :  * @param ppszResult the resulting string is returned in this pointer.
    1325             :  * @param papszOptions NULL terminated list of options, or NULL. Currently
    1326             :  * supported options are
    1327             :  * <ul>
    1328             :  * <li>MULTILINE=YES/NO. Defaults to NO.</li>
    1329             :  * <li>FORMAT=SFSQL/WKT1_SIMPLE/WKT1/WKT1_GDAL/WKT1_ESRI/WKT2_2015/WKT2_2018/WKT2/DEFAULT.
    1330             :  *     If SFSQL, a WKT1 string without AXIS, TOWGS84, AUTHORITY or EXTENSION
    1331             :  *     node is returned.
    1332             :  *     If WKT1_SIMPLE, a WKT1 string without AXIS, AUTHORITY or EXTENSION
    1333             :  *     node is returned.
    1334             :  *     WKT1 is an alias of WKT1_GDAL.
    1335             :  *     WKT2 will default to the latest revision implemented (currently WKT2_2018)
    1336             :  * </li>
    1337             :  * </ul>
    1338             :  *
    1339             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE if successful.
    1340             :  * @since GDAL 3.0
    1341             :  */

    1493             : /************************************************************************/
    1494             : /*                           importFromWkt()                            */
    1495             : /************************************************************************/
    1496             : 
    1497             : /**
    1498             :  * \brief Import from WKT string.
    1499             :  *
    1500             :  * This method will wipe the existing SRS definition, and
    1501             :  * reassign it based on the contents of the passed WKT string.  Only as
    1502             :  * much of the input string as needed to construct this SRS is consumed from
    1503             :  * the input string, and the input string pointer
    1504             :  * is then updated to point to the remaining (unused) input.
    1505             :  *
    1506             :  * Consult also the <a href="wktproblems.html">OGC WKT Coordinate System Issues</a> page
    1507             :  * for implementation details of WKT in OGR.
    1508             :  *
    1509             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRImportFromWkt().
    1510             :  *
    1511             :  * @param ppszInput Pointer to pointer to input.  The pointer is updated to
    1512             :  * point to remaining unused input text.
    1513             :  *
    1514             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE if import succeeds, or OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA if it
    1515             :  * fails for any reason.
    1516             :  * @since GDAL 2.3
    1517             :  */
    1518             : 
    1519       27945 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromWkt( const char ** ppszInput )
    1520             : 
    1521             : {
    1522       27945 :     if( !ppszInput || !*ppszInput )
    1523           2 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    1524             : 
    1525       27943 :     Clear();
    1526             : 
    1527       27943 :     if( **ppszInput )
    1528             :     {
    1529       27867 :         const char* const options[] = { "STRICT=NO", nullptr };
    1530       27867 :         PROJ_STRING_LIST warnings = nullptr;
    1531       27867 :         PROJ_STRING_LIST errors = nullptr;
    1532             :         d->setPjCRS(proj_create_from_wkt(
    1533       27867 :             d->getPROJContext(), *ppszInput, options, &warnings, &errors));
    1534       28214 :         for( auto iter = warnings; iter && *iter; ++iter ) {
    1535         347 :             d->m_wktImportWarnings.push_back(*iter);
    1536             :         }
    1537       27997 :         for( auto iter = errors; iter && *iter; ++iter ) {
    1538         130 :             d->m_wktImportErrors.push_back(*iter);
    1539         130 :             if( !d->m_pj_crs )
    1540             :             {
    1541          39 :                 CPLError(CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined, "%s", *iter);
    1542             :             }
    1543             :         }
    1544       27867 :         proj_string_list_destroy(warnings);
    1545       27867 :         proj_string_list_destroy(errors);
    1546             :     }
    1547       27943 :     if( !d->m_pj_crs )
    1548         115 :         return OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA;
    1549             : 
    1550             :     // Only accept CRS objects
    1551       27828 :     const auto type = d->m_pjType;
    1552       27828 :     if( type != PJ_TYPE_GEODETIC_CRS &&
    1553       27818 :         type != PJ_TYPE_GEOCENTRIC_CRS &&
    1554       17511 :         type != PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_2D_CRS &&
    1555       17480 :         type != PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_3D_CRS &&
    1556       17480 :         type != PJ_TYPE_VERTICAL_CRS &&
    1557        1191 :         type != PJ_TYPE_PROJECTED_CRS &&
    1558        1134 :         type != PJ_TYPE_COMPOUND_CRS &&
    1559        1134 :         type != PJ_TYPE_TEMPORAL_CRS &&
    1560         966 :         type != PJ_TYPE_ENGINEERING_CRS &&
    1561           0 :         type != PJ_TYPE_BOUND_CRS &&
    1562             :         type != PJ_TYPE_OTHER_CRS )
    1563             :     {
    1564           0 :         Clear();
    1565           0 :         return OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA;
    1566             :     }
    1567             :

    1600             : /**
    1601             :  * \brief Import from WKT string.
    1602             :  *
    1603             :  * This method will wipe the existing SRS definition, and
    1604             :  * reassign it based on the contents of the passed WKT string.  Only as
    1605             :  * much of the input string as needed to construct this SRS is consumed from
    1606             :  * the input string, and the input string pointer
    1607             :  * is then updated to point to the remaining (unused) input.
    1608             :  *
    1609             :  * Consult also the <a href="wktproblems.html">OGC WKT Coordinate System Issues</a> page
    1610             :  * for implementation details of WKT in OGR.
    1611             :  *
    1612             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRImportFromWkt().
    1613             :  *
    1614             :  * @param ppszInput Pointer to pointer to input.  The pointer is updated to
    1615             :  * point to remaining unused input text.
    1616             :  *
    1617             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE if import succeeds, or OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA if it
    1618             :  * fails for any reason.
    1619             :  * @deprecated GDAL 2.3. Use importFromWkt(const char**) or importFromWkt(const char*)
    1620             :  */
    1621             : 
    1622           0 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromWkt( char ** ppszInput )
    1623             : 
    1624             : {
    1625           0 :     return importFromWkt( const_cast<const char**>(ppszInput) );
    1626             : }

    1633             :  * much of the input string as needed to construct this SRS is consumed from
    1634             :  * the input string, and the input string pointer
    1635             :  * is then updated to point to the remaining (unused) input.
    1636             :  *
    1637             :  * Consult also the <a href="wktproblems.html">OGC WKT Coordinate System Issues</a> page
    1638             :  * for implementation details of WKT in OGR.

    1652             : /************************************************************************/
    1653             : /*                              Validate()                              */

    1655             : 
    1656             : /**
    1657             :  * \brief Validate CRS imported with importFromWkt() or with modified with
    1658             :  * direct node manipulations. Otherwise the CRS should be always valid.
    1659             :  *
    1660             :  * This method attempts to verify that the spatial reference system is
    1661             :  * well formed, and consists of known tokens.  The validation is not
    1662             :  * comprehensive.
    1663             :  *
    1664             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRValidate().
    1665             :  *
    1666             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE if all is fine, OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA if the SRS is
    1667             :  * not well formed, and OGRERR_UNSUPPORTED_SRS if the SRS is well formed,
    1668             :  * but contains non-standard PROJECTION[] values.

    1677             :     }
    1678         117 :     for( const auto& str: d->m_wktImportWarnings )
    1679             :     {
    1680           0 :         CPLDebug("OGRSpatialReference::Validate", "%s", str.c_str());

    1682         117 :     if( !d->m_pj_crs || !d->m_wktImportErrors.empty() )
    1683             :     {
    1684          37 :         return OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA;
    1685             :     }
    1686          80 :     if( !d->m_wktImportWarnings.empty() )
    1687             :     {
    1688           0 :         return OGRERR_UNSUPPORTED_SRS;
    1689             :     }
    1690          80 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    1691             : }

    1735             : /**

    1886             :  * units to transform to radians.  Some standard conversion factors can

    1940             : 
    1941             : /**
    1942             :  * \brief Fetch angular geographic coordinate system units.
    1943             :  *
    1944             :  * If no units are available, a value of "degree" and SRS_UA_DEGREE_CONV
    1945             :  * will be assumed.  This method only checks directly under the GEOGCS node
    1946             :  * for units.

    1948             :  * This method does the same thing as the C function OSRGetAngularUnits().
    1949             :  *
    1950             :  * @param ppszName a pointer to be updated with the pointer to the units name.
    1951             :  * The returned value remains internal to the OGRSpatialReference and should
    1952             :  * not be freed, or modified.  It may be invalidated on the next
    1953             :  * OGRSpatialReference call.
    1954             :  *
    1955             :  * @return the value to multiply by angular distances to transform them to
    1956             :  * radians.

    2071             : /************************************************************************/
    2072             : /*                 SetLinearUnitsAndUpdateParameters()                  */
    2073             : /************************************************************************/
    2074             : 
    2075             : /**
    2076             :  * \brief Set the linear units for the projection.
    2077             :  *
    2078             :  * This method creates a UNIT subnode with the specified values as a
    2079             :  * child of the PROJCS or LOCAL_CS node.   It works the same as the
    2080             :  * SetLinearUnits() method, but it also updates all existing linear
    2081             :  * projection parameter values from the old units to the new units.
    2082             :  *
    2083             :  * @param pszName the units name to be used.  Some preferred units

    2576             : /**

    2594             : /************************************************************************/
    2595             : /*                          GetPrimeMeridian()                          */
    2596             : /************************************************************************/
    2597             : 
    2598             : /**
    2599             :  * \brief Fetch prime meridian info.
    2600             :  *
    2601             :  * Returns the offset of the prime meridian from greenwich in degrees,
    2602             :  * and the prime meridian name (if requested).   If no PRIMEM value exists
    2603             :  * in the coordinate system definition a value of "Greenwich" and an
    2604             :  * offset of 0.0 is assumed.
    2605             :  *
    2606             :  * If the prime meridian name is returned, the pointer is to an internal
    2607             :  * copy of the name. It should not be freed, altered or depended on after
    2608             :  * the next OGR call.
    2609             :  *
    2610             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRGetPrimeMeridian().
    2611             :  *
    2612             :  * @param ppszName return location for prime meridian name.  If NULL, name
    2613             :  * is not returned.
    2614             :  *
    2615             :  * @return the offset to the GEOGCS prime meridian from greenwich in decimal
    2616             :  * degrees.
    2617             :  * @deprecated GDAL 2.3.0. Use GetPrimeMeridian(const char**) const.
    2618             :  */
    2619             : 
    2620         990 : double OGRSpatialReference::GetPrimeMeridian( const char **ppszName ) const
    2621             : 
    2622             : {
    2623         990 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    2624             : 
    2625         990 :     if( !d->m_osPrimeMeridianName.empty() )
    2626             :     {
    2627          39 :         if( ppszName != nullptr )
    2628           1 :             *ppszName = d->m_osPrimeMeridianName.c_str();
    2629          39 :         return d->dfFromGreenwich;
    2630             :     }
    2631             : 
    2632             :     while(true)
    2633             :     {
    2634         951 :         if( !d->m_pj_crs) 
    2635           0 :             break;
    2636             : 
    2637             :         auto pm = proj_get_prime_meridian(
    2638         951 :             d->getPROJContext(), d->m_pj_crs);
    2639         951 :         if( !pm )
    2640           0 :             break;
    2641             : 
    2642         951 :         d->m_osPrimeMeridianName = proj_get_name(pm);
    2643         951 :         if( ppszName )
    2644           5 :             *ppszName = d->m_osPrimeMeridianName.c_str();
    2645         951 :         double dfLongitude = 0.0;
    2646         951 :         double dfConvFactor = 0.0;
    2647             :         proj_prime_meridian_get_parameters(
    2648         951 :             d->getPROJContext(), pm, &dfLongitude, &dfConvFactor, nullptr);
    2649         951 :         proj_destroy(pm);
    2650         951 :         d->dfFromGreenwich = dfLongitude * dfConvFactor / CPLAtof(SRS_UA_DEGREE_CONV);
    2651         951 :         return d->dfFromGreenwich;
    2652             :     }
    2653             : 
    2654           0 :     d->m_osPrimeMeridianName = SRS_PM_GREENWICH;
    2655           0 :     d->dfFromGreenwich = 0.0;
    2656           0 :     if( ppszName != nullptr )
    2657           0 :         *ppszName = d->m_osPrimeMeridianName.c_str();
    2658           0 :     return d->dfFromGreenwich;
    2659             : }
    2660             : 
    2661             : /**
    2662             :  * \brief Fetch prime meridian info.
    2663             :  *
    2664             :  * Returns the offset of the prime meridian from greenwich in degrees,
    2665             :  * and the prime meridian name (if requested).   If no PRIMEM value exists
    2666             :  * in the coordinate system definition a value of "Greenwich" and an
    2667             :  * offset of 0.0 is assumed.
    2668             :  *
    2669             :  * If the prime meridian name is returned, the pointer is to an internal
    2670             :  * copy of the name. It should not be freed, altered or depended on after
    2671             :  * the next OGR call.
    2672             :  *
    2673             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRGetPrimeMeridian().
    2674             :  *
    2675             :  * @param ppszName return location for prime meridian name.  If NULL, name
    2676             :  * is not returned.
    2677             :  *

    2679             :  * degrees.
    2680             :  * @since GDAL 2.3.0
    2681             :  */
    2682             :

    2707             : /************************************************************************/
    2708             : /*                             SetGeogCS()                              */
    2709             : /************************************************************************/
    2710             : 
    2711             : /**
    2712             :  * \brief Set geographic coordinate system.
    2713             :  *
    2714             :  * This method is used to set the datum, ellipsoid, prime meridian and
    2715             :  * angular units for a geographic coordinate system.  It can be used on its
    2716             :  * own to establish a geographic spatial reference, or applied to a
    2717             :  * projected coordinate system to establish the underlying geographic
    2718             :  * coordinate system.
    2719             :  *
    2720             :  * This method does the same as the C function OSRSetGeogCS().
    2721             :  *
    2722             :  * @param pszGeogName user visible name for the geographic coordinate system
    2723             :  * (not to serve as a key).
    2724             :  *
    2725             :  * @param pszDatumName key name for this datum.  The OpenGIS specification
    2726             :  * lists some known values, and otherwise EPSG datum names with a standard
    2727             :  * transformation are considered legal keys.
    2728             :  *
    2729             :  * @param pszSpheroidName user visible spheroid name (not to serve as a key)
    2730             :  *
    2731             :  * @param dfSemiMajor the semi major axis of the spheroid.
    2732             :  *
    2733             :  * @param dfInvFlattening the inverse flattening for the spheroid.
    2734             :  * This can be computed from the semi minor axis as
    2735             :  * 1/f = 1.0 / (1.0 - semiminor/semimajor).
    2736             :  *
    2737             :  * @param pszPMName the name of the prime meridian (not to serve as a key)
    2738             :  * If this is NULL a default value of "Greenwich" will be used.
    2739             :  *
    2740             :  * @param dfPMOffset the longitude of Greenwich relative to this prime
    2741             :  * meridian. Always in Degrees
    2742             :  *
    2743             :  * @param pszAngularUnits the angular units name (see ogr_srs_api.h for some
    2744             :  * standard names).  If NULL a value of "degrees" will be assumed.
    2745             :  *
    2746             :  * @param dfConvertToRadians value to multiply angular units by to transform
    2747             :  * them to radians.  A value of SRS_UA_DEGREE_CONV will be used if
    2748             :  * pszAngularUnits is NULL.
    2749             :  *
    2750             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success.
    2751             :  */
    2752             : 
    2753             : OGRErr
    2754        3290 : OGRSpatialReference::SetGeogCS( const char * pszGeogName,
    2755             :                                 const char * pszDatumName,
    2756             :                                 const char * pszSpheroidName,
    2757             :                                 double dfSemiMajor, double dfInvFlattening,
    2758             :                                 const char * pszPMName, double dfPMOffset,
    2759             :                                 const char * pszAngularUnits,
    2760             :                                 double dfConvertToRadians )
    2761             : 
    2762             : {
    2763        3290 :     d->bNormInfoSet = FALSE;
    2764        3290 :     d->m_osAngularUnits.clear();
    2765        3290 :     d->m_dfAngularUnitToRadian = 0.0;
    2766        3290 :     d->m_osPrimeMeridianName.clear();
    2767        3290 :     d->dfFromGreenwich = 0.0;
    2768             : 
    2769             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2770             : /*      For a geocentric coordinate system we want to set the datum     */
    2771             : /*      and ellipsoid based on the GEOGCS.  Create the GEOGCS in a      */
    2772             : /*      temporary srs and use the copy method which has special         */
    2773             : /*      handling for GEOCCS.                                            */
    2774             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2775        3290 :     if( IsGeocentric() )
    2776             :     {
    2777           2 :         OGRSpatialReference oGCS;
    2778             : 
    2779             :         oGCS.SetGeogCS( pszGeogName, pszDatumName, pszSpheroidName,
    2780             :                         dfSemiMajor, dfInvFlattening,
    2781             :                         pszPMName, dfPMOffset,
    2782           2 :                         pszAngularUnits, dfConvertToRadians );
    2783           2 :         return CopyGeogCSFrom( &oGCS );
    2784             :     }
    2785             : 
    2786             :     auto cs = proj_create_ellipsoidal_2D_cs(
    2787             :         d->getPROJContext(),PJ_ELLPS2D_LATITUDE_LONGITUDE,
    2788        3288 :         pszAngularUnits, dfConvertToRadians);
    2789             :     // Prime meridian expressed in Degree
    2790             :     auto obj = proj_create_geographic_crs(
    2791             :         d->getPROJContext(),pszGeogName, pszDatumName, pszSpheroidName,
    2792             :         dfSemiMajor, dfInvFlattening, pszPMName, dfPMOffset,
    2793        3288 :         nullptr, 0.0, cs);
    2794        3288 :     proj_destroy(cs);
    2795             : 
    2796        8855 :     if( d->m_pj_crs == nullptr ||
    2797        5566 :         d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_2D_CRS ||
    2798        2278 :         d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_3D_CRS )
    2799             :     {
    2800        1010 :         d->setPjCRS(obj);
    2801             :     }
    2802        2278 :     else if( d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_PROJECTED_CRS )
    2803             :     {
    2804             :         d->setPjCRS(
    2805             :             proj_crs_alter_geodetic_crs(
    2806        2278 :                 d->getPROJContext(), d->m_pj_crs, obj));
    2807        2278 :         proj_destroy(obj);
    2808             :     }
    2809             :     else
    2810             :     {
    2811           0 :         proj_destroy(obj);
    2812             :     }
    2813             : 
    2814        3288 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    2815             : }

    2818             : /*                            OSRSetGeogCS()                            */
    2819             : /************************************************************************/
    2820             : 
    2821             : /**
    2822             :  * \brief Set geographic coordinate system.
    2823             :  *
    2824             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::SetGeogCS()

    2826          24 : OGRErr OSRSetGeogCS( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS,
    2827             :                      const char * pszGeogName,

    2831             :                      const char * pszPMName, double dfPMOffset,

    2834             : 
    2835             : {
    2836          24 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRSetGeogCS", OGRERR_FAILURE );

    2839             :         pszGeogName, pszDatumName,

    2844             : /************************************************************************/
    2845             : /*                         SetWellKnownGeogCS()                         */
    2846             : /************************************************************************/
    2847             : 
    2848             : /**
    2849             :  * \brief Set a GeogCS based on well known name.
    2850             :  *
    2851             :  * This may be called on an empty OGRSpatialReference to make a geographic
    2852             :  * coordinate system, or on something with an existing PROJCS node to
    2853             :  * set the underlying geographic coordinate system of a projected coordinate
    2854             :  * system.
    2855             :  *
    2856             :  * The following well known text values are currently supported:
    2857             :  * <ul>
    2858             :  * <li> "WGS84": same as "EPSG:4326" but has no dependence on EPSG data files.
    2859             :  * <li> "WGS72": same as "EPSG:4322" but has no dependence on EPSG data files.
    2860             :  * <li> "NAD27": same as "EPSG:4267" but has no dependence on EPSG data files.
    2861             :  * <li> "NAD83": same as "EPSG:4269" but has no dependence on EPSG data files.
    2862             :  * <li> "EPSG:n": where n is the code a Geographic coordinate reference system.
    2863             :  * </ul>
    2864             :  *
    2865             :  * @param pszName name of well known geographic coordinate system.
    2866             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success, or OGRERR_FAILURE if the name isn't
    2867             :  * recognised, the target object is already initialized, or an EPSG value
    2868             :  * can't be successfully looked up.
    2869             :  */
    2870             : 
    2871        1663 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::SetWellKnownGeogCS( const char * pszName )
    2872             : 
    2873             : {
    2874             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2875             : /*      Check for EPSG authority numbers.                               */
    2876             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2877        1663 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszName, "EPSG:") || STARTS_WITH_CI(pszName, "EPSGA:") )
    2878             :     {
    2879          32 :         OGRSpatialReference oSRS2;
    2880          32 :         const OGRErr eErr = oSRS2.importFromEPSG( atoi(pszName+5) );
    2881          32 :         if( eErr != OGRERR_NONE )
    2882           0 :             return eErr;
    2883             : 
    2884          32 :         if( !oSRS2.IsGeographic() )
    2885           0 :             return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    2886             : 
    2887          32 :         return CopyGeogCSFrom( &oSRS2 );
    2888             :     }
    2889             : 
    2890             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2891             : /*      Check for simple names.                                         */
    2892             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2893        1631 :     const char *pszWKT = nullptr;
    2894             : 
    2895        1631 :     if( EQUAL(pszName, "WGS84") )
    2896             :     {
    2897        1292 :         pszWKT = SRS_WKT_WGS84_LAT_LONG;
    2898             :     }
    2899         668 :     else if( EQUAL(pszName, "CRS84") ||
    2900         329 :              EQUAL(pszName, "CRS:84") )
    2901             :     {
    2902          11 :         pszWKT = "GEOGCS[\"WGS 84\",DATUM[\"WGS_1984\",SPHEROID[\"WGS 84\",6378137,298.257223563,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"7030\"]],AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"6326\"]],PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"8901\"]],UNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"9122\"]],AXIS[\"Longitude\",EAST],AXIS[\"Latitude\",NORTH]]";
    2903             :     }
    2904         328 :     else if( EQUAL(pszName, "WGS72") )
    2905             :         pszWKT =
    2906             :             "GEOGCS[\"WGS 72\",DATUM[\"WGS_1972\","
    2907             :             "SPHEROID[\"WGS 72\",6378135,298.26,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"7043\"]],"
    2908             :             "TOWGS84[0,0,4.5,0,0,0.554,0.2263],AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"6322\"]],"
    2909             :             "PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"8901\"]],"
    2910             :             "UNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"9122\"]],AXIS[\"Latitude\",NORTH],AXIS[\"Longitude\",EAST],"
    2911          27 :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"4322\"]]";
    2912             : 
    2913         301 :     else if( EQUAL(pszName, "NAD27") )
    2914             :         pszWKT =
    2915             :             "GEOGCS[\"NAD27\",DATUM[\"North_American_Datum_1927\","
    2916             :             "SPHEROID[\"Clarke 1866\",6378206.4,294.9786982138982,"
    2917             :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"7008\"]],AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"6267\"]],"
    2918             :             "PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"8901\"]],"
    2919             :             "UNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"9122\"]],AXIS[\"Latitude\",NORTH],AXIS[\"Longitude\",EAST],"
    2920         150 :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"4267\"]]";
    2921             : 
    2922         151 :     else if( EQUAL(pszName, "CRS27") || EQUAL(pszName, "CRS:27") )
    2923             :         pszWKT =
    2924             :             "GEOGCS[\"NAD27\",DATUM[\"North_American_Datum_1927\","
    2925             :             "SPHEROID[\"Clarke 1866\",6378206.4,294.9786982138982,"
    2926             :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"7008\"]],AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"6267\"]],"
    2927             :             "PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"8901\"]],"
    2928           0 :             "UNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433,AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"9122\"]],AXIS[\"Longitude\",EAST],AXIS[\"Latitude\",NORTH]]";
    2929             : 
    2930         151 :     else if( EQUAL(pszName, "NAD83") )
    2931             :         pszWKT =
    2932             :             "GEOGCS[\"NAD83\",DATUM[\"North_American_Datum_1983\","
    2933             :             "SPHEROID[\"GRS 1980\",6378137,298.257222101,"
    2934             :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"7019\"]],"
    2935             :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"6269\"]],PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,"
    2936             :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"8901\"]],UNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433,"
    2937         148 :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"9122\"]],AXIS[\"Latitude\",NORTH],AXIS[\"Longitude\",EAST],AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"4269\"]]";
    2938             : 
    2939           3 :     else if(  EQUAL(pszName, "CRS83") ||  EQUAL(pszName, "CRS:83") )
    2940             :         pszWKT =
    2941             :             "GEOGCS[\"NAD83\",DATUM[\"North_American_Datum_1983\","
    2942             :             "SPHEROID[\"GRS 1980\",6378137,298.257222101,"
    2943             :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"7019\"]],"
    2944             :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"6269\"]],PRIMEM[\"Greenwich\",0,"
    2945             :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"8901\"]],UNIT[\"degree\",0.0174532925199433,"
    2946           0 :             "AUTHORITY[\"EPSG\",\"9122\"]],AXIS[\"Longitude\",EAST],AXIS[\"Latitude\",NORTH]]";
    2947             : 
    2948             :     else
    2949           3 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    2950             : 
    2951             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2952             : /*      Import the WKT                                                  */
    2953             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2954        1628 :     OGRSpatialReference oSRS2;
    2955        1628 :     const OGRErr eErr = oSRS2.importFromWkt( pszWKT );
    2956        1628 :     if( eErr != OGRERR_NONE )
    2957           0 :         return eErr;
    2958             : 
    2959             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2960             : /*      Copy over.                                                      */
    2961             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    2962        1628 :     return CopyGeogCSFrom( &oSRS2 );
    2963             : }
    2964             : 
    2965             : /************************************************************************/
    2966             : /*                       OSRSetWellKnownGeogCS()                        */
    2967             : /************************************************************************/
    2968             : 
    2969             : /**
    2970             :  * \brief Set a GeogCS based on well known name.
    2971             :  *
    2972             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::SetWellKnownGeogCS()
    2973             :  */
    2974         102 : OGRErr OSRSetWellKnownGeogCS( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS, const char *pszName )
    2975             : 
    2976             : {
    2977         102 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRSetWellKnownGeogCS", OGRERR_FAILURE );

    2980         102 :         SetWellKnownGeogCS( pszName );

    2983             : /************************************************************************/
    2984             : /*                           CopyGeogCSFrom()                           */
    2985             : /************************************************************************/
    2986             : 
    2987             : /**
    2988             :  * \brief Copy GEOGCS from another OGRSpatialReference.
    2989             :  *
    2990             :  * The GEOGCS information is copied into this OGRSpatialReference from another.
    2991             :  * If this object has a PROJCS root already, the GEOGCS is installed within
    2992             :  * it, otherwise it is installed as the root.
    2993             :  *
    2994             :  * @param poSrcSRS the spatial reference to copy the GEOGCS information from.
    2995             :  *
    2996             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success or an error code.
    2997             :  */
    2998             : 
    2999        1973 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::CopyGeogCSFrom(
    3000             :     const OGRSpatialReference * poSrcSRS )
    3001             : 
    3002             : {
    3003        1973 :     d->bNormInfoSet = FALSE;
    3004        1973 :     d->m_osAngularUnits.clear();
    3005        1973 :     d->m_dfAngularUnitToRadian = 0.0;
    3006        1973 :     d->m_osPrimeMeridianName.clear();
    3007        1973 :     d->dfFromGreenwich = 0.0;
    3008             : 
    3009        1973 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    3010        1973 :     poSrcSRS->d->refreshProjObj();
    3011        1973 :     if( !poSrcSRS->d->m_pj_crs )
    3012             :     {
    3013           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3014             :     }
    3015             :     auto geodCRS = proj_crs_get_geodetic_crs(
    3016        1973 :         d->getPROJContext(),poSrcSRS->d->m_pj_crs);
    3017        1973 :     if( !geodCRS )
    3018             :     {
    3019           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3020             :     }
    3021             : 
    3022             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3023             : /*      Handle geocentric coordinate systems specially.  We just        */
    3024             : /*      want to copy the DATUM.                                         */
    3025             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3026        1973 :     if( d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_GEOCENTRIC_CRS )
    3027             :     {
    3028             :         auto datum = proj_crs_get_datum(
    3029           3 :             d->getPROJContext(), geodCRS);
    3030           3 :         CPLAssert(datum);
    3031             : 
    3032           3 :         const char* pszUnitName = nullptr;
    3033           3 :         double unitConvFactor = GetLinearUnits(&pszUnitName);
    3034             : 
    3035             :         auto pj_crs = proj_create_geocentric_crs_from_datum(
    3036             :             d->getPROJContext(),
    3037           3 :             proj_get_name(d->m_pj_crs), datum, pszUnitName, unitConvFactor);
    3038           3 :         proj_destroy(datum);
    3039             : 
    3040           3 :         d->setPjCRS(pj_crs);
    3041             :     }
    3042             : 
    3043        1970 :     else if( d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_PROJECTED_CRS )
    3044             :     {
    3045             :         auto pj_crs = proj_crs_alter_geodetic_crs(
    3046         293 :             d->getPROJContext(), d->m_pj_crs, geodCRS);
    3047         293 :         d->setPjCRS(pj_crs);
    3048             :     }
    3049             : 
    3050             :     else
    3051             :     {
    3052        1677 :         d->setPjCRS(proj_clone(d->getPROJContext(), geodCRS));
    3053             :     }
    3054             : 
    3055             :     // Apply TOWGS84 of source CRS
    3056        1973 :     if( poSrcSRS->d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_BOUND_CRS )
    3057             :     {
    3058             :         auto target = proj_get_target_crs(
    3059          85 :             d->getPROJContext(), poSrcSRS->d->m_pj_crs);
    3060             :         auto co = proj_crs_get_coordoperation(
    3061          85 :             d->getPROJContext(), poSrcSRS->d->m_pj_crs);

    3064          85 :         proj_destroy(target);
    3065          85 :         proj_destroy(co);
    3066             :     }
    3067             : 
    3068        1973 :     proj_destroy(geodCRS);
    3069             : 
    3070        1973 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    3071             : }

    3092             : /************************************************************************/
    3093             : /*                          SetFromUserInput()                          */
    3094             : /************************************************************************/
    3095             : 
    3096             : /**
    3097             :  * \brief Set spatial reference from various text formats.
    3098             :  *
    3099             :  * This method will examine the provided input, and try to deduce the
    3100             :  * format, and then use it to initialize the spatial reference system.  It
    3101             :  * may take the following forms:
    3102             :  *
    3103             :  * <ol>
    3104             :  * <li> Well Known Text definition - passed on to importFromWkt().
    3105             :  * <li> "EPSG:n" - number passed on to importFromEPSG().
    3106             :  * <li> "EPSGA:n" - number passed on to importFromEPSGA().
    3107             :  * <li> "AUTO:proj_id,unit_id,lon0,lat0" - WMS auto projections.
    3108             :  * <li> "urn:ogc:def:crs:EPSG::n" - ogc urns
    3109             :  * <li> PROJ.4 definitions - passed on to importFromProj4().
    3110             :  * <li> filename - file read for WKT, XML or PROJ.4 definition.
    3111             :  * <li> well known name accepted by SetWellKnownGeogCS(), such as NAD27, NAD83,
    3112             :  * WGS84 or WGS72.
    3113             :  * <li> "IGNF:xxxx", "ESRI:xxxx", etc. from definitions from the PROJ database;
    3114             :  * </ol>
    3115             :  *
    3116             :  * It is expected that this method will be extended in the future to support
    3117             :  * XML and perhaps a simplified "minilanguage" for indicating common UTM and
    3118             :  * State Plane definitions.
    3119             :  *
    3120             :  * This method is intended to be flexible, but by its nature it is
    3121             :  * imprecise as it must guess information about the format intended.  When
    3122             :  * possible applications should call the specific method appropriate if the
    3123             :  * input is known to be in a particular format.
    3124             :  *
    3125             :  * This method does the same thing as the OSRSetFromUserInput() function.
    3126             :  *
    3127             :  * @param pszDefinition text definition to try to deduce SRS from.
    3128             :  *
    3129             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success, or an error code if the name isn't
    3130             :  * recognised, the definition is corrupt, or an EPSG value can't be
    3131             :  * successfully looked up.
    3132             :  */
    3133             : 
    3134       23680 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::SetFromUserInput( const char * pszDefinition )
    3135             : 
    3136             : {
    3137       23680 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "ESRI::") )
    3138             :     {
    3139           1 :         pszDefinition += 6;
    3140             :     }
    3141             : 
    3142             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3143             : /*      Is it a recognised syntax?                                      */
    3144             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3145             :     const char* const wktKeywords[] = {
    3146             :         // WKT1
    3147             :         "GEOGCS", "GEOCCS", "PROJCS", "VERT_CS", "COMPD_CS", "LOCAL_CS",
    3148             :         // WKT2"
    3149             :         "GEODCRS", "GEOGCRS", "GEODETICCRS", "GEOGRAPHICCRS", "PROJCRS",
    3150             :         "PROJECTEDCRS", "VERTCRS", "VERTICALCRS", "COMPOUNDCRS",
    3151             :         "ENGCRS", "ENGINEERINGCRS", "BOUNDCRS"
    3152       23680 :     };
    3153      175983 :     for( const char* keyword: wktKeywords )
    3154             :     {
    3155      168896 :         if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, keyword) )
    3156             :         {
    3157       16593 :             return importFromWkt( pszDefinition );
    3158             :         }
    3159             :     }
    3160             : 
    3161        7087 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "EPSG:")
    3162        1135 :         || STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "EPSGA:") )
    3163             :     {
    3164        6010 :         OGRErr eStatus = OGRERR_NONE;
    3165             : 
    3166        6010 :         if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "EPSG:") )
    3167        5952 :             eStatus = importFromEPSG( atoi(pszDefinition+5) );
    3168             : 
    3169             :         else // if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "EPSGA:") )
    3170          58 :             eStatus = importFromEPSGA( atoi(pszDefinition+6) );
    3171             : 
    3172             :         // Do we want to turn this into a compound definition
    3173             :         // with a vertical datum?
    3174        6010 :         if( eStatus == OGRERR_NONE && strchr( pszDefinition, '+' ) != nullptr )
    3175             :         {
    3176          27 :             OGRSpatialReference oVertSRS;
    3177             : 
    3178             :             eStatus = oVertSRS.importFromEPSG(
    3179          27 :                 atoi(strchr(pszDefinition, '+') + 1) );
    3180          27 :             if( eStatus == OGRERR_NONE )
    3181             :             {
    3182          27 :                 OGRSpatialReference oHorizSRS(*this);
    3183             : 
    3184          27 :                 Clear();
    3185             : 
    3186          27 :                 oHorizSRS.d->refreshProjObj();
    3187          27 :                 oVertSRS.d->refreshProjObj();
    3188          27 :                 if( !oHorizSRS.d->m_pj_crs || !oVertSRS.d->m_pj_crs )
    3189           0 :                     return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3190             : 
    3191          27 :                 const char* pszHorizName = proj_get_name(oHorizSRS.d->m_pj_crs);
    3192          27 :                 const char* pszVertName = proj_get_name(oVertSRS.d->m_pj_crs);
    3193             : 
    3194          54 :                 CPLString osName = pszHorizName ? pszHorizName : "";
    3195          27 :                 osName += " + ";
    3196          27 :                 osName += pszVertName ? pszVertName : "";
    3197             : 
    3198          54 :                 SetCompoundCS(osName, &oHorizSRS, &oVertSRS);
    3199          27 :             }
    3200             :         }
    3201             : 
    3202        6010 :         return eStatus;
    3203             :     }
    3204             : 
    3205        1077 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "urn:ogc:def:crs:")
    3206         555 :         || STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "urn:ogc:def:crs,crs:")
    3207         554 :         || STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "urn:x-ogc:def:crs:")
    3208         515 :         || STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "urn:opengis:crs:")
    3209         515 :         || STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "urn:opengis:def:crs:"))
    3210         562 :         return importFromURN( pszDefinition );
    3211             : 
    3212         515 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "http://opengis.net/def/crs")
    3213         515 :         || STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "http://www.opengis.net/def/crs")
    3214         461 :         || STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "www.opengis.net/def/crs"))
    3215          54 :         return importFromCRSURL( pszDefinition );
    3216             : 
    3217         461 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "AUTO:") )
    3218           0 :         return importFromWMSAUTO( pszDefinition );
    3219             : 
    3220             :     // WMS/WCS OGC codes like OGC:CRS84.
    3221         461 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "OGC:") )
    3222           0 :         return SetWellKnownGeogCS( pszDefinition+4 );
    3223             : 
    3224         461 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "CRS:") )
    3225           1 :         return SetWellKnownGeogCS( pszDefinition );
    3226             : 
    3227         460 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "DICT:")
    3228           0 :         && strstr(pszDefinition, ",") )
    3229             :     {
    3230           0 :         char *pszFile = CPLStrdup(pszDefinition+5);
    3231           0 :         char *pszCode = strstr(pszFile, ",") + 1;
    3232             : 
    3233           0 :         pszCode[-1] = '\0';
    3234             : 
    3235           0 :         OGRErr err = importFromDict( pszFile, pszCode );
    3236           0 :         CPLFree( pszFile );
    3237             : 
    3238           0 :         return err;
    3239             :     }
    3240             : 
    3241         460 :     if( EQUAL(pszDefinition, "NAD27")
    3242         458 :         || EQUAL(pszDefinition,"NAD83")
    3243         458 :         || EQUAL(pszDefinition,"WGS84")
    3244         315 :         || EQUAL(pszDefinition,"WGS72") )
    3245             :     {
    3246         154 :         Clear();
    3247         154 :         return SetWellKnownGeogCS( pszDefinition );
    3248             :     }
    3249             : 
    3250         306 :     if( strstr(pszDefinition, "+proj") != nullptr
    3251          54 :              || strstr(pszDefinition, "+init") != nullptr )
    3252         252 :         return importFromProj4( pszDefinition );
    3253             : 
    3254          54 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "http://") )
    3255             :     {
    3256           0 :         return importFromUrl (pszDefinition);
    3257             :     }
    3258             : 
    3259          54 :     if( EQUAL(pszDefinition, "osgb:BNG") )
    3260             :     {
    3261           6 :         return importFromEPSG(27700);
    3262             :     }
    3263             : 
    3264             : 
    3265             :     // Deal with IGNF:xxx, ESRI:xxx, etc from the PROJ database
    3266          48 :     const char* pszDot = strchr(pszDefinition, ':');
    3267          48 :     if( pszDot )
    3268             :     {
    3269          11 :         CPLString osPrefix(pszDefinition, pszDot - pszDefinition);
    3270          11 :         auto authorities = proj_get_authorities_from_database(d->getPROJContext());
    3271          11 :         if( authorities )

    3273          40 :             for( auto iter = authorities; *iter; ++iter )
    3274             :             {
    3275          37 :                 if( *iter == osPrefix )
    3276             :                 {
    3277           8 :                     proj_string_list_destroy(authorities);
    3278             : 
    3279             :                     auto obj = proj_create_from_database(d->getPROJContext(),
    3280             :                         osPrefix, pszDot + 1, PJ_CATEGORY_CRS,
    3281           8 :                         false, nullptr);

    3288           7 :                     return OGRERR_NONE;
    3289             :                 }
    3290             :             }
    3291           3 :             proj_string_list_destroy(authorities);
    3292           3 :         }
    3293             :     }
    3294             : 
    3295             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3296             : /*      Try to open it as a file.                                       */
    3297             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */

    3300          40 :     if( fp == nullptr )
    3301          35 :         return OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA;

    3303           5 :     const size_t nBufMax = 100000;

    3305           5 :     const size_t nBytes = VSIFReadL( pszBuffer, 1, nBufMax-1, fp );
    3306           5 :     VSIFCloseL( fp );
    3307             : 
    3308           5 :     if( nBytes == nBufMax-1 )
    3309             :     {
    3310             :         CPLDebug( "OGR",
    3311             :                   "OGRSpatialReference::SetFromUserInput(%s), opened file "
    3312             :                   "but it is to large for our generous buffer.  Is it really "
    3313           0 :                   "just a WKT definition?", pszDefinition );
    3314           0 :         CPLFree( pszBuffer );
    3315           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3316             :     }

    3351             :

    3366             : /************************************************************************/
    3367             : /*                          ImportFromUrl()                             */
    3368             : /************************************************************************/
    3369             : 
    3370             : /**
    3371             :  * \brief Set spatial reference from a URL.
    3372             :  *
    3373             :  * This method will download the spatial reference at a given URL and
    3374             :  * feed it into SetFromUserInput for you.
    3375             :  *
    3376             :  * This method does the same thing as the OSRImportFromUrl() function.
    3377             :  *
    3378             :  * @param pszUrl text definition to try to deduce SRS from.
    3379             :  *
    3380             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success, or an error code with the curl
    3381             :  * error message if it is unable to dowload data.
    3382             :  */
    3383             : 
    3384           4 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromUrl( const char * pszUrl )
    3385             : 
    3386             : {
    3387           4 :     if( !STARTS_WITH_CI(pszUrl, "http://") )
    3388             :     {
    3389             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3390             :                   "The given string is not recognized as a URL"
    3391           2 :                   "starting with 'http://' -- %s", pszUrl );
    3392           2 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3393             :     }
    3394             : 
    3395             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3396             : /*      Fetch the result.                                               */
    3397             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3398           2 :     CPLErrorReset();
    3399             : 
    3400           2 :     const char* pszHeaders = "HEADERS=Accept: application/x-ogcwkt";
    3401           2 :     const char* pszTimeout = "TIMEOUT=10";
    3402             :     char *apszOptions[] = {
    3403             :         const_cast<char *>(pszHeaders),
    3404             :         const_cast<char *>(pszTimeout),
    3405             :         nullptr
    3406           2 :     };
    3407             : 
    3408           2 :     CPLHTTPResult *psResult = CPLHTTPFetch( pszUrl, apszOptions );
    3409             : 
    3410             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3411             : /*      Try to handle errors.                                           */
    3412             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3413             : 
    3414           2 :     if( psResult == nullptr )
    3415           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3416           4 :     if( psResult->nDataLen == 0
    3417           2 :         || CPLGetLastErrorNo() != 0 || psResult->pabyData == nullptr )
    3418             :     {
    3419           0 :         if( CPLGetLastErrorNo() == 0 )
    3420             :         {
    3421             :             CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3422           0 :                     "No data was returned from the given URL" );
    3423             :         }
    3424           0 :         CPLHTTPDestroyResult( psResult );
    3425           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3426             :     }
    3427             : 
    3428           2 :     if( psResult->nStatus != 0 )
    3429             :     {
    3430             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3431             :                   "Curl reports error: %d: %s",
    3432           0 :                   psResult->nStatus, psResult->pszErrBuf );
    3433           0 :         CPLHTTPDestroyResult( psResult );
    3434           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3435             :     }
    3436             : 
    3437           2 :     const char* pszData = reinterpret_cast<const char*>(psResult->pabyData);
    3438           2 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszData, "http://") )
    3439             :     {
    3440             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3441             :                   "The data that was downloaded also starts with 'http://' "
    3442             :                   "and cannot be passed into SetFromUserInput.  Is this "
    3443           0 :                   "really a spatial reference definition? ");
    3444           0 :         CPLHTTPDestroyResult( psResult );
    3445           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3446             :     }
    3447           2 :     if( OGRERR_NONE != SetFromUserInput(pszData)) {
    3448           0 :         CPLHTTPDestroyResult( psResult );
    3449           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3450             :     }
    3451             : 
    3452           2 :     CPLHTTPDestroyResult( psResult );
    3453           2 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    3454             : }

    3456             : /************************************************************************/
    3457             : /*                        OSRimportFromUrl()                            */
    3458             : /************************************************************************/
    3459             : 
    3460             : /**
    3461             :  * \brief Set spatial reference from a URL.
    3462             :  *
    3463             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::importFromUrl()
    3464             :  */
    3465           2 : OGRErr OSRImportFromUrl( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS, const char *pszUrl )
    3466             : 
    3467             : {
    3468           2 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRImportFromUrl", OGRERR_FAILURE );
    3469             :

    3474             : /************************************************************************/
    3475             : /*                         importFromURNPart()                          */
    3476             : /************************************************************************/
    3477         673 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromURNPart(const char* pszAuthority,
    3478             :                                               const char* pszCode,
    3479             :                                               const char* pszURN)
    3480             : {
    3481             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3482             : /*      Is this an EPSG code? Note that we import it with EPSG          */
    3483             : /*      preferred axis ordering for geographic coordinate systems.      */
    3484             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3485         673 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszAuthority, "EPSG") )
    3486         661 :         return importFromEPSGA( atoi(pszCode) );
    3487             : 
    3488             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3489             : /*      Is this an IAU code?  Lets try for the IAU2000 dictionary.      */
    3490             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3491          12 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszAuthority, "IAU") )
    3492           0 :         return importFromDict( "IAU2000.wkt", pszCode );
    3493             : 
    3494             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3495             : /*      Is this an OGC code?                                            */
    3496             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3497          12 :     if( !STARTS_WITH_CI(pszAuthority, "OGC") )
    3498             :     {
    3499             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3500             :                   "URN %s has unrecognized authority.",
    3501           0 :                   pszURN );
    3502           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3503             :     }
    3504             : 
    3505          12 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszCode, "CRS84") )
    3506           6 :         return SetWellKnownGeogCS( pszCode );
    3507           6 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszCode, "CRS83") )
    3508           0 :         return SetWellKnownGeogCS( pszCode );
    3509           6 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszCode, "CRS27") )
    3510           0 :         return SetWellKnownGeogCS( pszCode );
    3511           6 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszCode, "84") )  // urn:ogc:def:crs:OGC:2:84
    3512           4 :         return SetWellKnownGeogCS( "CRS84" );
    3513             : 
    3514             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3515             : /*      Handle auto codes.  We need to convert from format              */
    3516             : /*      AUTO42001:99:8888 to format AUTO:42001,99,8888.                 */
    3517             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3518           2 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszCode, "AUTO") )
    3519             :     {
    3520           2 :         char szWMSAuto[100] = { '\0' };
    3521             : 
    3522           2 :         if( strlen(pszCode) > sizeof(szWMSAuto)-2 )
    3523           0 :             return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3524             : 
    3525           2 :         snprintf( szWMSAuto, sizeof(szWMSAuto), "AUTO:%s", pszCode + 4 );
    3526          28 :         for( int i = 5; szWMSAuto[i] != '\0'; i++ )
    3527             :         {
    3528          26 :             if( szWMSAuto[i] == ':' )
    3529           4 :                 szWMSAuto[i] = ',';
    3530             :         }
    3531             : 
    3532           2 :         return importFromWMSAUTO( szWMSAuto );
    3533             :     }
    3534             : 
    3535             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3536             : /*      Not a recognise OGC item.                                       */
    3537             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3538             :     CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3539             :               "URN %s value not supported.",
    3540           0 :               pszURN );
    3541             : 
    3542           0 :     return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3543             : }
    3544             : 
    3545             : /************************************************************************/
    3546             : /*                           importFromURN()                            */
    3547             : /*                                                                      */
    3548             : /*      See OGC recommendation paper 06-023r1 or later for details.     */
    3549             : /************************************************************************/
    3550             : 
    3551             : /**
    3552             :  * \brief Initialize from OGC URN.
    3553             :  *
    3554             :  * Initializes this spatial reference from a coordinate system defined
    3555             :  * by an OGC URN prefixed with "urn:ogc:def:crs:" per recommendation
    3556             :  * paper 06-023r1.  Currently EPSG and OGC authority values are supported,
    3557             :  * including OGC auto codes, but not including CRS1 or CRS88 (NAVD88).
    3558             :  *
    3559             :  * This method is also support through SetFromUserInput() which can
    3560             :  * normally be used for URNs.
    3561             :  *
    3562             :  * @param pszURN the urn string.
    3563             :  *
    3564             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success or an error code.
    3565             :  */
    3566             : 
    3567         615 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromURN( const char *pszURN )
    3568             : 
    3569             : {
    3570         615 :     const char *pszCur = nullptr;
    3571             : 
    3572         615 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszURN, "urn:ogc:def:crs:") )
    3573         566 :         pszCur = pszURN + 16;
    3574          49 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszURN, "urn:ogc:def:crs,crs:") )
    3575           1 :         pszCur = pszURN + 20;
    3576          48 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszURN, "urn:x-ogc:def:crs:") )
    3577          46 :         pszCur = pszURN + 18;
    3578           2 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszURN, "urn:opengis:crs:") )
    3579           0 :         pszCur = pszURN + 16;
    3580           2 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszURN, "urn:opengis:def:crs:") )
    3581           0 :         pszCur = pszURN + 20;
    3582             :     else
    3583             :     {
    3584             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3585           2 :                   "URN %s not a supported format.", pszURN );
    3586           2 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3587             :     }
    3588             : 
    3589             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3590             : /*      Clear any existing definition.                                  */
    3591             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3592         613 :     Clear();
    3593             : 
    3594             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3595             : /*      Find code (ignoring version) out of string like:                */
    3596             : /*                                                                      */
    3597             : /*      authority:[version]:code                                        */
    3598             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3599         613 :     const char *pszAuthority = pszCur;
    3600             : 
    3601             :     // skip authority
    3602        3666 :     while( *pszCur != ':' && *pszCur )
    3603        2440 :         pszCur++;
    3604         613 :     if( *pszCur == ':' )
    3605         613 :         pszCur++;
    3606             : 
    3607             :     // skip version
    3608         613 :     const char* pszBeforeVersion = pszCur;
    3609        1429 :     while( *pszCur != ':' && *pszCur )
    3610         203 :         pszCur++;
    3611         613 :     if( *pszCur == ':' )
    3612         604 :         pszCur++;
    3613             :     else
    3614             :         // We come here in the case, the content to parse is authority:code
    3615             :         // (instead of authority::code) which is probably illegal according to
    3616             :         // http://www.opengeospatial.org/ogcUrnPolicy but such content is found
    3617             :         // for example in what is returned by GeoServer.
    3618           9 :         pszCur = pszBeforeVersion;
    3619             : 
    3620         613 :     const char *pszCode = pszCur;
    3621             : 
    3622         613 :     const char* pszComma = strchr(pszCur, ',');
    3623         613 :     if( pszComma == nullptr )
    3624         612 :         return importFromURNPart(pszAuthority, pszCode, pszURN);
    3625             : 
    3626             :     // There's a second part with the vertical SRS.
    3627           1 :     pszCur = pszComma + 1;
    3628           1 :     if( !STARTS_WITH(pszCur, "crs:") )
    3629             :     {
    3630             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3631           0 :                   "URN %s not a supported format.", pszURN );
    3632           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3633             :     }
    3634             : 
    3635           1 :     pszCur += 4;
    3636             : 
    3637           1 :     char* pszFirstCode = CPLStrdup(pszCode);
    3638           1 :     pszFirstCode[pszComma - pszCode] = '\0';
    3639           1 :     OGRErr eStatus = importFromURNPart(pszAuthority, pszFirstCode, pszURN);
    3640           1 :     CPLFree(pszFirstCode);
    3641             : 
    3642             :     // Do we want to turn this into a compound definition
    3643             :     // with a vertical datum?
    3644           1 :     if( eStatus != OGRERR_NONE )
    3645           0 :         return eStatus;
    3646             : 
    3647             :     /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3648             :     /*      Find code (ignoring version) out of string like:                */
    3649             :     /*                                                                      */
    3650             :     /*      authority:[version]:code                                        */
    3651             :     /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3652           1 :     pszAuthority = pszCur;
    3653             : 
    3654             :     // skip authority
    3655           6 :     while( *pszCur != ':' && *pszCur )
    3656           4 :         pszCur++;
    3657           1 :     if( *pszCur == ':' )
    3658           1 :         pszCur++;
    3659             : 
    3660             :     // skip version
    3661           1 :     pszBeforeVersion = pszCur;
    3662           2 :     while( *pszCur != ':' && *pszCur )
    3663           0 :         pszCur++;
    3664           1 :     if( *pszCur == ':' )
    3665           1 :         pszCur++;
    3666             :     else
    3667           0 :         pszCur = pszBeforeVersion;
    3668             : 
    3669           1 :     pszCode = pszCur;
    3670             : 
    3671           1 :     OGRSpatialReference oVertSRS;
    3672           1 :     eStatus = oVertSRS.importFromURNPart(pszAuthority, pszCode, pszURN);
    3673           1 :     if( eStatus == OGRERR_NONE )
    3674             :     {
    3675           1 :         OGRSpatialReference oHorizSRS(*this);
    3676             : 
    3677           1 :         Clear();
    3678             : 
    3679           1 :         oHorizSRS.d->refreshProjObj();
    3680           1 :         oVertSRS.d->refreshProjObj();
    3681           1 :         if( !oHorizSRS.d->m_pj_crs || !oVertSRS.d->m_pj_crs )
    3682           0 :             return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3683             : 
    3684           1 :         const char* pszHorizName = proj_get_name(oHorizSRS.d->m_pj_crs);
    3685           1 :         const char* pszVertName = proj_get_name(oVertSRS.d->m_pj_crs);
    3686             : 
    3687           2 :         CPLString osName = pszHorizName ? pszHorizName : "";
    3688           1 :         osName += " + ";
    3689           1 :         osName += pszVertName ? pszVertName : "";
    3690             : 
    3691           2 :         SetCompoundCS(osName, &oHorizSRS, &oVertSRS);
    3692             :     }
    3693             : 
    3694           1 :     return eStatus;
    3695             : }
