    3697             : /************************************************************************/
    3698             : /*                           importFromCRSURL()                         */
    3699             : /*                                                                      */
    3700             : /*      See OGC Best Practice document 11-135 for details.              */
    3701             : /************************************************************************/
    3702             : 
    3703             : /**
    3704             :  * \brief Initialize from OGC URL.
    3705             :  *
    3706             :  * Initializes this spatial reference from a coordinate system defined
    3707             :  * by an OGC URL prefixed with "http://opengis.net/def/crs" per best practice
    3708             :  * paper 11-135.  Currently EPSG and OGC authority values are supported,
    3709             :  * including OGC auto codes, but not including CRS1 or CRS88 (NAVD88).
    3710             :  *
    3711             :  * This method is also supported through SetFromUserInput() which can
    3712             :  * normally be used for URLs.
    3713             :  *
    3714             :  * @param pszURL the URL string.
    3715             :  *
    3716             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success or an error code.
    3717             :  */
    3718             : 
    3719          60 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromCRSURL( const char *pszURL )
    3720             : 
    3721             : {
    3722          60 :     const char *pszCur = nullptr;
    3723             : 
    3724          60 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszURL, "http://opengis.net/def/crs") )
    3725           0 :         pszCur = pszURL + 26;
    3726          60 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszURL, "http://www.opengis.net/def/crs") )
    3727          60 :         pszCur = pszURL + 30;
    3728           0 :     else if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszURL, "www.opengis.net/def/crs") )
    3729           0 :         pszCur = pszURL + 23;
    3730             :     else
    3731             :     {
    3732             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3733           0 :                   "URL %s not a supported format.", pszURL );
    3734           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3735             :     }
    3736             : 
    3737          60 :     if( *pszCur == '\0' )
    3738             :     {
    3739           0 :         CPLError(CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined, "URL %s malformed.", pszURL);
    3740           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3741             :     }
    3742             : 
    3743             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3744             : /*      Clear any existing definition.                                  */
    3745             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3746          60 :     Clear();
    3747             : 
    3748          60 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszCur, "-compound?1=") )
    3749             :     {
    3750             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3751             : /*      It's a compound CRS, of the form:                               */
    3752             : /*                                                                      */
    3753             : /*      http://opengis.net/def/crs-compound?1=URL1&2=URL2&3=URL3&..     */
    3754             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3755           1 :         pszCur += 12;
    3756             : 
    3757             :         // Extract each component CRS URL.
    3758           1 :         int iComponentUrl = 2;
    3759             : 
    3760           1 :         CPLString osName = "";
    3761           1 :         Clear();
    3762             : 
    3763           4 :         while( iComponentUrl != -1 )
    3764             :         {
    3765           2 :             char searchStr[15] = {};
    3766           2 :             snprintf(searchStr, sizeof(searchStr), "&%d=", iComponentUrl);
    3767             : 
    3768           2 :             const char* pszUrlEnd = strstr(pszCur, searchStr);
    3769             : 
    3770             :             // Figure out the next component URL.
    3771           2 :             char* pszComponentUrl = nullptr;
    3772             : 
    3773           2 :             if( pszUrlEnd )
    3774             :             {
    3775           1 :                 size_t nLen = pszUrlEnd - pszCur;
    3776           1 :                 pszComponentUrl = static_cast<char *>(CPLMalloc(nLen + 1));
    3777           1 :                 strncpy(pszComponentUrl, pszCur, nLen);
    3778           1 :                 pszComponentUrl[nLen] = '\0';
    3779             : 
    3780           1 :                 ++iComponentUrl;
    3781           1 :                 pszCur += nLen + strlen(searchStr);
    3782             :             }
    3783             :             else
    3784             :             {
    3785           1 :                 if( iComponentUrl == 2 )
    3786             :                 {
    3787             :                     CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3788           0 :                               "Compound CRS URLs must have at least two component CRSs." );
    3789           0 :                     return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3790             :                 }
    3791             :                 else
    3792             :                 {
    3793           1 :                     pszComponentUrl = CPLStrdup(pszCur);
    3794             :                     // no more components
    3795           1 :                     iComponentUrl = -1;
    3796             :                 }
    3797             :             }
    3798             : 
    3799           2 :             OGRSpatialReference oComponentSRS;
    3800           2 :             OGRErr eStatus = oComponentSRS.importFromCRSURL( pszComponentUrl );
    3801             : 
    3802           2 :             CPLFree(pszComponentUrl);
    3803           2 :             pszComponentUrl = nullptr;
    3804             : 
    3805           2 :             if( eStatus == OGRERR_NONE )
    3806             :             {
    3807           2 :                 if( osName.length() != 0 )
    3808             :                 {
    3809           1 :                   osName += " + ";
    3810             :                 }
    3811           2 :                 osName += oComponentSRS.GetRoot()->GetValue();
    3812           2 :                 SetNode( "COMPD_CS", osName );
    3813           2 :                 GetRoot()->AddChild( oComponentSRS.GetRoot()->Clone() );
    3814             :             }
    3815             :             else
    3816           0 :                 return eStatus;
    3817           2 :         }
    3818             : 
    3819           1 :         return OGRERR_NONE;
    3820             :     }
    3821             : 
    3822             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3823             : /*      It's a normal CRS URL, of the form:                             */
    3824             : /*                                                                      */
    3825             : /*      http://opengis.net/def/crs/AUTHORITY/VERSION/CODE               */
    3826             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3827          59 :     ++pszCur;
    3828          59 :     const char *pszAuthority = pszCur;
    3829             : 
    3830             :     // skip authority
    3831         354 :     while( *pszCur != '/' && *pszCur )
    3832         236 :         pszCur++;
    3833          59 :     if( *pszCur == '/' )
    3834          59 :         pszCur++;
    3835             : 
    3836             :     // skip version
    3837         177 :     while( *pszCur != '/' && *pszCur )
    3838          59 :         pszCur++;
    3839          59 :     if( *pszCur == '/' )
    3840          59 :         pszCur++;
    3841             : 
    3842          59 :     const char *pszCode = pszCur;
    3843             : 
    3844          59 :     return importFromURNPart( pszAuthority, pszCode, pszURL );
    3845             : }
    3846             : 
    3847             : /************************************************************************/
    3848             : /*                         importFromWMSAUTO()                          */
    3849             : /************************************************************************/
    3850             : 
    3851             : /**
    3852             :  * \brief Initialize from WMSAUTO string.
    3853             :  *
    3854             :  * Note that the WMS 1.3 specification does not include the
    3855             :  * units code, while apparently earlier specs do.  We try to
    3856             :  * guess around this.
    3857             :  *
    3858             :  * @param pszDefinition the WMSAUTO string
    3859             :  *
    3860             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success or an error code.
    3861             :  */
    3862           2 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromWMSAUTO( const char * pszDefinition )
    3863             : 
    3864             : {
    3865             :     int nProjId, nUnitsId;
    3866           2 :     double dfRefLong, dfRefLat = 0.0;
    3867             : 
    3868             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3869             : /*      Tokenize                                                        */
    3870             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3871           2 :     if( STARTS_WITH_CI(pszDefinition, "AUTO:") )
    3872           2 :         pszDefinition += 5;
    3873             : 
    3874             :     char **papszTokens =
    3875           2 :         CSLTokenizeStringComplex( pszDefinition, ",", FALSE, TRUE );
    3876             : 
    3877           2 :     if( CSLCount(papszTokens) == 4 )
    3878             :     {
    3879           0 :         nProjId = atoi(papszTokens[0]);
    3880           0 :         nUnitsId = atoi(papszTokens[1]);
    3881           0 :         dfRefLong = CPLAtof(papszTokens[2]);
    3882           0 :         dfRefLat = CPLAtof(papszTokens[3]);
    3883             :     }
    3884           2 :     else if( CSLCount(papszTokens) == 3 && atoi(papszTokens[0]) == 42005 )
    3885             :     {
    3886           0 :         nProjId = atoi(papszTokens[0]);
    3887           0 :         nUnitsId = atoi(papszTokens[1]);
    3888           0 :         dfRefLong = CPLAtof(papszTokens[2]);
    3889           0 :         dfRefLat = 0.0;
    3890             :     }
    3891           2 :     else if( CSLCount(papszTokens) == 3 )
    3892             :     {
    3893           2 :         nProjId = atoi(papszTokens[0]);
    3894           2 :         nUnitsId = 9001;
    3895           2 :         dfRefLong = CPLAtof(papszTokens[1]);
    3896           2 :         dfRefLat = CPLAtof(papszTokens[2]);
    3897             :     }
    3898           0 :     else if( CSLCount(papszTokens) == 2 && atoi(papszTokens[0]) == 42005 )
    3899             :     {
    3900           0 :         nProjId = atoi(papszTokens[0]);
    3901           0 :         nUnitsId = 9001;
    3902           0 :         dfRefLong = CPLAtof(papszTokens[1]);
    3903             :     }
    3904             :     else
    3905             :     {
    3906           0 :         CSLDestroy( papszTokens );
    3907             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3908             :                   "AUTO projection has wrong number of arguments, expected\n"
    3909             :                   "AUTO:proj_id,units_id,ref_long,ref_lat or"
    3910           0 :                   "AUTO:proj_id,ref_long,ref_lat" );
    3911           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3912             :     }
    3913             : 
    3914           2 :     CSLDestroy( papszTokens );
    3915           2 :     papszTokens = nullptr;
    3916             : 
    3917             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3918             : /*      Build coordsys.                                                 */
    3919             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3920           2 :     Clear();
    3921             : 
    3922             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3923             : /*      Set WGS84.                                                      */
    3924             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3925           2 :     SetWellKnownGeogCS( "WGS84" );
    3926             : 
    3927           2 :     switch( nProjId )
    3928             :     {
    3929             :       case 42001: // Auto UTM
    3930           2 :         SetUTM( static_cast<int>(floor( (dfRefLong + 180.0) / 6.0 )) + 1,
    3931           4 :                 dfRefLat >= 0.0 );
    3932           2 :         break;
    3933             : 
    3934             :       case 42002: // Auto TM (strangely very UTM-like).
    3935             :         SetTM( 0, dfRefLong, 0.9996,
    3936           0 :                500000.0, (dfRefLat >= 0.0) ? 0.0 : 10000000.0 );
    3937           0 :         break;
    3938             : 
    3939             :       case 42003: // Auto Orthographic.
    3940           0 :         SetOrthographic( dfRefLat, dfRefLong, 0.0, 0.0 );
    3941           0 :         break;
    3942             : 
    3943             :       case 42004: // Auto Equirectangular
    3944           0 :         SetEquirectangular( dfRefLat, dfRefLong, 0.0, 0.0 );
    3945           0 :         break;
    3946             : 
    3947             :       case 42005:
    3948           0 :         SetMollweide( dfRefLong, 0.0, 0.0 );
    3949           0 :         break;
    3950             : 
    3951             :       default:
    3952             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3953             :                   "Unsupported projection id in importFromWMSAUTO(): %d",
    3954           0 :                   nProjId );
    3955           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3956             :     }
    3957             : 
    3958             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3959             : /*      Set units.                                                      */
    3960             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    3961             : 
    3962           2 :     switch( nUnitsId )
    3963             :     {
    3964             :       case 9001:
    3965           2 :         SetTargetLinearUnits( nullptr, SRS_UL_METER, 1.0, "EPSG", "9001" );
    3966           2 :         break;
    3967             : 
    3968             :       case 9002:
    3969           0 :         SetTargetLinearUnits( nullptr, "Foot", 0.3048, "EPSG", "9002" );
    3970           0 :         break;
    3971             : 
    3972             :       case 9003:
    3973           0 :         SetTargetLinearUnits( nullptr,  "US survey foot", CPLAtof(SRS_UL_US_FOOT_CONV), "EPSG", "9003" );
    3974           0 :         break;
    3975             : 
    3976             :       default:
    3977             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    3978             :                   "Unsupported units code (%d).",
    3979           0 :                   nUnitsId );
    3980           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    3981             :         break;
    3982             :     }
    3983             : 
    3984           2 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    3985             : }
    3986             : 
    3987             : /************************************************************************/
    3988             : /*                            GetSemiMajor()                            */
    3989             : /************************************************************************/
    3990             : 
    3991             : /**
    3992             :  * \brief Get spheroid semi major axis (in metres starting with GDAL 3.0)
    3993             :  *
    3994             :  * This method does the same thing as the C function OSRGetSemiMajor().
    3995             :  *
    3996             :  * @param pnErr if non-NULL set to OGRERR_FAILURE if semi major axis
    3997             :  * can be found.
    3998             :  *
    3999             :  * @return semi-major axis, or SRS_WGS84_SEMIMAJOR if it can't be found.
    4000             :  */
    4001             :

    4130           0 :         return 0.0;

    4133           0 :     return sqrt(2.0 / dfInvFlattening -
    4134           0 :                     1.0 / (dfInvFlattening * dfInvFlattening));
    4135             : }

    4262             : /*                             SetGeocCS()                              */
    4263             : /************************************************************************/
    4264             : 
    4265             : /**
    4266             :  * \brief Set the user visible GEOCCS name.
    4267             :  *
    4268             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRSetGeocCS().
    4269             : 
    4270             :  * This method will ensure a GEOCCS node is created as the root,
    4271             :  * and set the provided name on it.  If used on a GEOGCS coordinate system,
    4272             :  * the DATUM and PRIMEM nodes from the GEOGCS will be transferred over to
    4273             :  * the GEOGCS.
    4274             :  *
    4275             :  * @param pszName the user visible name to assign.  Not used as a key.
    4276             :  *

    4279             :  * @since OGR 1.9.0

    4283             : 
    4284             : {
    4285           6 :     OGRErr eErr = OGRERR_NONE;
    4286           6 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    4287           6 :     d->demoteFromBoundCRS();
    4288           6 :     if( d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_UNKNOWN )
    4289             :     {
    4290             :         d->setPjCRS(proj_create_geocentric_crs(
    4291             :             d->getPROJContext(),
    4292             :             pszName, "World Geodetic System 1984", "WGS 84", SRS_WGS84_SEMIMAJOR,
    4293             :             SRS_WGS84_INVFLATTENING, SRS_PM_GREENWICH, 0.0, SRS_UA_DEGREE,
    4294             :             CPLAtof(SRS_UA_DEGREE_CONV),
    4295           3 :             "Metre", 1.0));
    4296             :     }
    4297           3 :     else if( d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_GEOCENTRIC_CRS ) {
    4298             :         d->setPjCRS(proj_alter_name(
    4299           1 :             d->getPROJContext(),d->m_pj_crs, pszName));
    4300             :     }
    4301           3 :     else if( d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_2D_CRS ||
    4302           1 :              d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_3D_CRS )
    4303             :     {
    4304             :         auto datum = proj_crs_get_datum(
    4305           1 :             d->getPROJContext(), d->m_pj_crs);
    4306           1 :         CPLAssert(datum);
    4307             : 
    4308             :         auto pj_crs = proj_create_geocentric_crs_from_datum(
    4309             :             d->getPROJContext(),
    4310           1 :             proj_get_name(d->m_pj_crs), datum, nullptr, 0.0);
    4311           1 :         d->setPjCRS(pj_crs);
    4312             : 
    4313           1 :         proj_destroy(datum);
    4314             :     }
    4315             :     else
    4316             :     {
    4317             :         CPLDebug( "OGR",
    4318             :                 "OGRSpatialReference::SetGeocCS(%s) failed.  "
    4319             :                 "It appears an incompatible object already exists.",
    4320           1 :                 pszName );
    4321           1 :         eErr = OGRERR_FAILURE;
    4322             :     }
    4323           6 :     d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
    4324             : 
    4325           6 :     return eErr;
    4326             : }

    4347             : /************************************************************************/
    4348             : /*                             SetVertCS()                              */
    4349             : /************************************************************************/
    4350             : 
    4351             : /**
    4352             :  * \brief Set the user visible VERT_CS name.
    4353             :  *
    4354             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRSetVertCS().
    4355             : 
    4356             :  * This method will ensure a VERT_CS node is created if needed.  If the
    4357             :  * existing coordinate system is GEOGCS or PROJCS rooted, then it will be
    4358             :  * turned into a COMPD_CS.
    4359             :  *
    4360             :  * @param pszVertCSName the user visible name of the vertical coordinate
    4361             :  * system. Not used as a key.
    4362             :  *
    4363             :  * @param pszVertDatumName the user visible name of the vertical datum.  It
    4364             :  * is helpful if this matches the EPSG name.
    4365             :  *
    4366             :  * @param nVertDatumType the OGC vertical datum type. Ignored
    4367             :  *
    4368             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success.
    4369             :  *
    4370             :  * @since OGR 1.9.0
    4371             :  */
    4372             : 
    4373           0 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::SetVertCS( const char * pszVertCSName,
    4374             :                                        const char * pszVertDatumName,
    4375             :                                        int nVertDatumType )
    4376             : 
    4377             : {
    4378           0 :     CPL_IGNORE_RET_VAL(nVertDatumType);
    4379             : 
    4380           0 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    4381             : 
    4382             :     auto vertCRS = 
    4383             :         proj_create_vertical_crs(d->getPROJContext(), pszVertCSName,
    4384           0 :                                      pszVertDatumName, nullptr, 0.0);
    4385             : 
    4386             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    4387             : /*      Handle the case where we want to make a compound coordinate     */
    4388             : /*      system.                                                         */
    4389             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    4390           0 :     if( IsProjected() || IsGeographic() )
    4391             :     {
    4392             :         auto compoundCRS = proj_create_compound_crs(d->getPROJContext(),
    4393             :                                                         nullptr,
    4394           0 :                                                         d->m_pj_crs,
    4395           0 :                                                         vertCRS);
    4396           0 :         proj_destroy(vertCRS);
    4397           0 :         d->setPjCRS(compoundCRS);
    4398             :     }
    4399             :     else
    4400             :     {
    4401           0 :         d->setPjCRS(vertCRS);
    4402             :     }
    4403           0 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    4404             : }

    4429             : /************************************************************************/
    4430             : /*                           SetCompoundCS()                            */
    4431             : /************************************************************************/
    4432             : 
    4433             : /**
    4434             :  * \brief Setup a compound coordinate system.
    4435             :  *
    4436             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRSetCompoundCS().
    4437             : 
    4438             :  * This method is replace the current SRS with a COMPD_CS coordinate system
    4439             :  * consisting of the passed in horizontal and vertical coordinate systems.
    4440             :  *
    4441             :  * @param pszName the name of the compound coordinate system.
    4442             :  *
    4443             :  * @param poHorizSRS the horizontal SRS (PROJCS or GEOGCS).
    4444             :  *
    4445             :  * @param poVertSRS the vertical SRS (VERT_CS).
    4446             :  *
    4447             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success.
    4448             :  */
    4449             : 
    4450             : OGRErr
    4451          30 : OGRSpatialReference::SetCompoundCS( const char *pszName,
    4452             :                                     const OGRSpatialReference *poHorizSRS,
    4453             :                                     const OGRSpatialReference *poVertSRS )
    4454             : 
    4455             : {
    4456             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    4457             : /*      Verify these are legal horizontal and vertical coordinate       */
    4458             : /*      systems.                                                        */
    4459             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    4460          30 :     if( !poVertSRS->IsVertical() )
    4461             :     {
    4462             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    4463           0 :                   "SetCompoundCS() fails, vertical component is not VERT_CS." );
    4464           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    4465             :     }
    4466          60 :     if( !poHorizSRS->IsProjected()
    4467          30 :         && !poHorizSRS->IsGeographic() )
    4468             :     {
    4469             :         CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined,
    4470           0 :                   "SetCompoundCS() fails, horizontal component is not PROJCS or GEOGCS." );
    4471           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    4472             :     }
    4473             : 
    4474             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    4475             : /*      Replace with compound srs.                                      */
    4476             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    4477          30 :     Clear();
    4478             : 
    4479             :     auto compoundCRS = proj_create_compound_crs(
    4480             :         d->getPROJContext(), pszName,
    4481          30 :         poHorizSRS->d->m_pj_crs,
    4482          60 :         poVertSRS->d->m_pj_crs);
    4483          30 :     d->setPjCRS(compoundCRS);
    4484             : 
    4485          30 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    4486             : }
    4487             : 
    4488             : /************************************************************************/
    4489             : /*                          OSRSetCompoundCS()                          */
    4490             : /************************************************************************/
    4491             : 
    4492             : /**
    4493             :  * \brief Setup a compound coordinate system.
    4494             :  *
    4495             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::SetCompoundCS()
    4496             :  */
    4497           1 : OGRErr OSRSetCompoundCS( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS,
    4498             :                          const char *pszName,
    4499             :                          OGRSpatialReferenceH hHorizSRS,
    4500             :                          OGRSpatialReferenceH hVertSRS )
    4501             : 
    4502             : {
    4503           1 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRSetCompoundCS", OGRERR_FAILURE );
    4504           1 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hHorizSRS, "OSRSetCompoundCS", OGRERR_FAILURE );
    4505           1 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hVertSRS, "OSRSetCompoundCS", OGRERR_FAILURE );
    4506             : 
    4507             :     return ToPointer(hSRS)->
    4508             :         SetCompoundCS( pszName,
    4509           1 :                        ToPointer(hHorizSRS),

    4513             : /************************************************************************/
    4514             : /*                             SetProjCS()                              */
    4515             : /************************************************************************/
    4516             : 
    4517             : /**
    4518             :  * \brief Set the user visible PROJCS name.
    4519             :  *
    4520             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRSetProjCS().
    4521             :  *
    4522             :  * This method will ensure a PROJCS node is created as the root,
    4523             :  * and set the provided name on it.  If used on a GEOGCS coordinate system,

    4850             : 
    4851             : /**
    4852             :  * \brief Fetch a normalized projection parameter value.

    4854             :  * This method is the same as GetProjParm() except that the value of
    4855             :  * the parameter is "normalized" into degrees or meters depending on
    4856             :  * whether it is linear or angular.
    4857             :  *
    4858             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRGetNormProjParm().
    4859             :  *
    4860             :  * @param pszName the name of the parameter to fetch, from the set of
    4861             :  * SRS_PP codes in ogr_srs_api.h.
    4862             :  *
    4863             :  * @param dfDefaultValue the value to return if this parameter doesn't exist.
    4864             :  *

    4866             :  *
    4867             :  * @return value of parameter.
    4868             :  */
    4869             : 
    4870        2460 : double OGRSpatialReference::GetNormProjParm( const char * pszName,
    4871             :                                              double dfDefaultValue,

    4873             : 
    4874             : {
    4875        2460 :     GetNormInfo();
    4876             : 
    4877        2460 :     OGRErr nError = OGRERR_NONE;
    4878        2460 :     double dfRawResult = GetProjParm( pszName, dfDefaultValue, &nError );
    4879        2460 :     if( pnErr != nullptr )
    4880           0 :         *pnErr = nError;
    4881             : 
    4882             :     // If we got the default just return it unadjusted.
    4883        2460 :     if( nError != OGRERR_NONE )
    4884         136 :         return dfRawResult;
    4885             : 
    4886        2324 :     if( d->dfToDegrees != 1.0 && IsAngularParameter(pszName) )
    4887           4 :         dfRawResult *= d->dfToDegrees;
    4888             : 
    4889        2324 :     if( d->dfToMeter != 1.0 && IsLinearParameter( pszName ) )
    4890           5 :         return dfRawResult * d->dfToMeter;
    4891             : 
    4892        2319 :     return dfRawResult;
    4893             : }

    4914             : /************************************************************************/
    4915             : /*                          SetNormProjParm()                           */
    4916             : /************************************************************************/
    4917             : 
    4918             : /**
    4919             :  * \brief Set a projection parameter with a normalized value.
    4920             :  *
    4921             :  * This method is the same as SetProjParm() except that the value of
    4922             :  * the parameter passed in is assumed to be in "normalized" form (decimal
    4923             :  * degrees for angular values, meters for linear values.  The values are
    4924             :  * converted in a form suitable for the GEOGCS and linear units in effect.
    4925             :  *
    4926             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRSetNormProjParm().
    4927             :  *
    4928             :  * @param pszName the parameter name, which should be selected from
    4929             :  * the macros in ogr_srs_api.h, such as SRS_PP_CENTRAL_MERIDIAN.
    4930             :  *
    4931             :  * @param dfValue value to assign.
    4932             :  *
    4933             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success.
    4934             :  */
    4935             : 
    4936          91 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::SetNormProjParm( const char * pszName,
    4937             :                                              double dfValue )
    4938             : 
    4939             : {
    4940          91 :     GetNormInfo();
    4941             : 
    4942         273 :     if( d->dfToDegrees != 0.0 &&
    4943         182 :         (d->dfToDegrees != 1.0 || d->dfFromGreenwich != 0.0)
    4944          91 :         && IsAngularParameter(pszName) )
    4945             :     {
    4946           0 :         dfValue /= d->dfToDegrees;
    4947             :     }
    4948          95 :     else if( d->dfToMeter != 1.0 && d->dfToMeter != 0.0 &&
    4949           4 :              IsLinearParameter( pszName ) )
    4950           4 :         dfValue /= d->dfToMeter;
    4951             : 
    4952          91 :     return SetProjParm( pszName, dfValue );
    4953             : }
    4954             :

    5835             : 
    5836             : /**
    5837             :  * \brief Set an Hotine Oblique Mercator Azimuth Center projection using
    5838             :  * azimuth angle.
    5839             :  *
    5840             :  * This projection corresponds to EPSG projection method 9815, also
    5841             :  * sometimes known as hotine oblique mercator (variant B).
    5842             :  *
    5843             :  * This method does the same thing as the C function OSRSetHOMAC().
    5844             :  *
    5845             :  * @param dfCenterLat Latitude of the projection origin.
    5846             :  * @param dfCenterLong Longitude of the projection origin.
    5847             :  * @param dfAzimuth Azimuth, measured clockwise from North, of the projection
    5848             :  * centerline.
    5849             :  * @param dfRectToSkew Angle from Rectified to Skew Grid
    5850             :  * @param dfScale Scale factor applies to the projection origin.
    5851             :  * @param dfFalseEasting False easting.
    5852             :  * @param dfFalseNorthing False northing.
    5853             :  *
    5854             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success.
    5855             :  */
    5856             :

    6098             : 
    6099             : /** Krovak east-north projection.
    6100             :  *
    6101             :  * Note that dfAzimuth and dfPseudoStdParallel1 are ignored when exporting
    6102             :  * to PROJ and should be respectively set to 30.28813972222222 and 78.5
    6103             :  */
    6104           3 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::SetKrovak( double dfCenterLat, double dfCenterLong,
    6105             :                                        double dfAzimuth,
    6106             :                                        double dfPseudoStdParallel1,
    6107             :                                        double dfScale,
    6108             :                                        double dfFalseEasting,
    6109             :                                        double dfFalseNorthing )
    6110             : 
    6111             : {

    6873             : /**
    6874             :  * \brief Set UTM projection definition.
    6875             :  *
    6876             :  * This will generate a projection definition with the full set of
    6877             :  * transverse mercator projection parameters for the given UTM zone.
    6878             :  * If no PROJCS[] description is set yet, one will be set to look
    6879             :  * like "UTM Zone %d, {Northern, Southern} Hemisphere".
    6880             :  *
    6881             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRSetUTM().
    6882             :  *
    6883             :  * @param nZone UTM zone.
    6884             :  *
    6885             :  * @param bNorth TRUE for northern hemisphere, or FALSE for southern
    6886             :  * hemisphere.
    6887             :  *
    6888             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success.
    6889             :  */
    6890             :

    6892             : 
    6893             : {
    6894         335 :     if( nZone < 0 || nZone > 60 )
    6895             :     {
    6896           0 :         CPLError(CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined, "Invalid zone: %d", nZone);
    6897           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    6898             :     }
    6899             : 
    6900             :     return d->replaceConversionAndUnref(
    6901         335 :         proj_create_conversion_utm(d->getPROJContext(), nZone, bNorth));

    6921             : /************************************************************************/
    6922             : /*                             GetUTMZone()                             */
    6923             : /*                                                                      */
    6924             : /*      Returns zero if it isn't UTM.                                   */
    6925             : /************************************************************************/
    6926             : 
    6927             : /**
    6928             :  * \brief Get utm zone information.
    6929             :  *
    6930             :  * This is the same as the C function OSRGetUTMZone().
    6931             :  *
    6932             :  * In SWIG bindings (Python, Java, etc) the GetUTMZone() method returns a
    6933             :  * zone which is negative in the southern hemisphere instead of having the
    6934             :  * pbNorth flag used in the C and C++ interface.
    6935             :  *
    6936             :  * @param pbNorth pointer to in to set to TRUE if northern hemisphere, or
    6937             :  * FALSE if southern.
    6938             :  *
    6939             :  * @return UTM zone number or zero if this isn't a UTM definition.
    6940             :  */
    6941             : 
    6942         424 : int OGRSpatialReference::GetUTMZone( int * pbNorth ) const
    6943             : 
    6944             : {
    6945         424 :     const char *pszProjection = GetAttrValue( "PROJECTION" );
    6946             : 
    6947         424 :     if( pszProjection == nullptr
    6948         386 :         || !EQUAL(pszProjection, SRS_PT_TRANSVERSE_MERCATOR) )
    6949         152 :         return 0;
    6950             : 
    6951         272 :     if( GetNormProjParm( SRS_PP_LATITUDE_OF_ORIGIN, 0.0 ) != 0.0 )
    6952           5 :         return 0;
    6953             : 
    6954         267 :     if( GetProjParm( SRS_PP_SCALE_FACTOR, 1.0 ) != 0.9996 )
    6955           7 :         return 0;
    6956             : 
    6957         260 :     if( fabs(GetNormProjParm( SRS_PP_FALSE_EASTING, 0.0 )-500000.0) > 0.001 )
    6958           3 :         return 0;
    6959             : 
    6960         257 :     const double dfFalseNorthing = GetNormProjParm( SRS_PP_FALSE_NORTHING, 0.0);
    6961             : 
    6962         257 :     if( dfFalseNorthing != 0.0
    6963          21 :         && fabs(dfFalseNorthing-10000000.0) > 0.001 )
    6964           0 :         return 0;
    6965             : 
    6966         257 :     if( pbNorth != nullptr )
    6967         208 :         *pbNorth = (dfFalseNorthing == 0);
    6968             : 
    6969             :     const double dfCentralMeridian =
    6970         257 :         GetNormProjParm( SRS_PP_CENTRAL_MERIDIAN, 0.0);
    6971         257 :     const double dfZone = (dfCentralMeridian + 186.0) / 6.0;
    6972             : 
    6973         514 :     if( dfCentralMeridian < -177.00001 ||
    6974         257 :         dfCentralMeridian > 177.000001 ||
    6975         514 :         CPLIsNan(dfZone) ||
    6976         257 :         std::abs(dfZone - static_cast<int>(dfZone) - 0.5 ) > 0.00001 )
    6977           0 :       return 0;
    6978             : 
    6979         257 :     return static_cast<int>(dfZone);
    6980             : }

    7147             : 
    7148             : /**
    7149             :  * \brief Set the authority for a node.
    7150             :  *
    7151             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRSetAuthority().
    7152             :  *
    7153             :  * @param pszTargetKey the partial or complete path to the node to
    7154             :  * set an authority on.  i.e. "PROJCS", "GEOGCS" or "GEOGCS|UNIT".
    7155             :  *
    7156             :  * @param pszAuthority authority name, such as "EPSG".
    7157             :  *
    7158             :  * @param nCode code for value with this authority.
    7159             :  *
    7160             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success.
    7161             :  */
    7162             : 
    7163        7433 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::SetAuthority( const char *pszTargetKey,
    7164             :                                           const char * pszAuthority,
    7165             :                                           int nCode )
    7166             : 
    7167             : {
    7168        7433 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    7169        7433 :     pszTargetKey = d->nullifyTargetKeyIfPossible(pszTargetKey);
    7170             : 
    7171        7433 :     if( pszTargetKey == nullptr )
    7172             :     {
    7173         971 :         if( !d->m_pj_crs )

    7275             : /************************************************************************/
    7276             : /*                          GetAuthorityCode()                          */
    7277             : /************************************************************************/
    7278             : 
    7279             : /**
    7280             :  * \brief Get the authority code for a node.
    7281             :  *
    7282             :  * This method is used to query an AUTHORITY[] node from within the
    7283             :  * WKT tree, and fetch the code value.
    7284             :  *
    7285             :  * While in theory values may be non-numeric, for the EPSG authority all
    7286             :  * code values should be integral.
    7287             :  *
    7288             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRGetAuthorityCode().
    7289             :  *
    7290             :  * @param pszTargetKey the partial or complete path to the node to
    7291             :  * get an authority from.  i.e. "PROJCS", "GEOGCS", "GEOGCS|UNIT" or NULL to
    7292             :  * search for an authority node on the root element.
    7293             :  *
    7294             :  * @return value code from authority node, or NULL on failure.  The value
    7295             :  * returned is internal and should not be freed or modified.
    7296             :  */
    7297             : 
    7298             : const char *
    7299       15451 : OGRSpatialReference::GetAuthorityCode( const char *pszTargetKey ) const
    7300             : 
    7301             : {
    7302       15451 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    7303       15451 :     pszTargetKey = d->nullifyTargetKeyIfPossible(pszTargetKey);
    7304       15451 :     if( pszTargetKey == nullptr )
    7305             :     {
    7306       11642 :         if( !d->m_pj_crs )
    7307             :         {
    7308           1 :             return nullptr;
    7309             :         }
    7310       11641 :         d->demoteFromBoundCRS();
    7311       11641 :         auto ret = proj_get_id_code(d->m_pj_crs, 0);
    7312       11641 :         d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
    7313       11641 :         return ret;
    7314             :     }
    7315             : 
    7316             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7317             : /*      Find the node below which the authority should be put.          */
    7318             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7319        3809 :     const OGR_SRSNode *poNode = GetAttrNode( pszTargetKey );
    7320             : 
    7321        3809 :     if( poNode == nullptr )
    7322          19 :         return nullptr;
    7323             : 
    7324             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7325             : /*      Fetch AUTHORITY child if there is one.                          */
    7326             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7327        3790 :     if( poNode->FindChild("AUTHORITY") == -1 )
    7328         100 :         return nullptr;
    7329             : 
    7330        3690 :     poNode = poNode->GetChild(poNode->FindChild("AUTHORITY"));
    7331             : 
    7332             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7333             : /*      Create a new authority node.                                    */
    7334             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7335        3690 :     if( poNode->GetChildCount() < 2 )
    7336           0 :         return nullptr;
    7337             : 
    7338        3690 :     return poNode->GetChild(1)->GetValue();
    7339             : }

    7362             : /************************************************************************/
    7363             : 
    7364             : /**
    7365             :  * \brief Get the authority name for a node.
    7366             :  *
    7367             :  * This method is used to query an AUTHORITY[] node from within the
    7368             :  * WKT tree, and fetch the authority name value.
    7369             :  *
    7370             :  * The most common authority is "EPSG".
    7371             :  *
    7372             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRGetAuthorityName().
    7373             :  *
    7374             :  * @param pszTargetKey the partial or complete path to the node to
    7375             :  * get an authority from.  i.e. "PROJCS", "GEOGCS", "GEOGCS|UNIT" or NULL to
    7376             :  * search for an authority node on the root element.
    7377             :  *

    7379             :  * returned is internal and should not be freed or modified.
    7380             :  */
    7381             : 
    7382             : const char *
    7383       23356 : OGRSpatialReference::GetAuthorityName( const char *pszTargetKey ) const
    7384             : 
    7385             : {
    7386       23356 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    7387       23356 :     pszTargetKey = d->nullifyTargetKeyIfPossible(pszTargetKey);
    7388       23356 :     if( pszTargetKey == nullptr )
    7389             :     {
    7390       10864 :         if( !d->m_pj_crs )
    7391             :         {
    7392           2 :             return nullptr;
    7393             :         }
    7394       10862 :         d->demoteFromBoundCRS();
    7395       10862 :         auto ret = proj_get_id_auth_name(d->m_pj_crs, 0);
    7396       10862 :         d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
    7397       10862 :         return ret;
    7398             :     }
    7399             : 
    7400             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7401             : /*      Find the node below which the authority should be put.          */
    7402             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7403       12492 :     const OGR_SRSNode *poNode = GetAttrNode( pszTargetKey );
    7404             : 
    7405       12492 :     if( poNode == nullptr )
    7406        4379 :         return nullptr;
    7407             : 
    7408             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7409             : /*      Fetch AUTHORITY child if there is one.                          */
    7410             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    7411        8113 :     if( poNode->FindChild("AUTHORITY") == -1 )
    7412        1196 :         return nullptr;
    7413             : 
    7414        6917 :     poNode = poNode->GetChild(poNode->FindChild("AUTHORITY"));
    7415             : 
    7416             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */

    7418             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */

    7444             : /************************************************************************/
    7445             : /*                           StripVertical()                            */
    7446             : /************************************************************************/
    7447             : 
    7448             : /**
    7449             :  * \brief Convert a compound cs into a horizontal CS.
    7450             :  *
    7451             :  * If this SRS is of type COMPD_CS[] then the vertical CS and the root COMPD_CS
    7452             :  * nodes are stripped resulting and only the horizontal coordinate system
    7453             :  * portion remains (normally PROJCS, GEOGCS or LOCAL_CS).
    7454             :  *
    7455             :  * If this is not a compound coordinate system then nothing is changed.
    7456             :  *
    7457             :  * @since OGR 1.8.0
    7458             :  */
    7459             : 
    7460          79 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::StripVertical()
    7461             : 
    7462             : {
    7463          79 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    7464          79 :     d->demoteFromBoundCRS();
    7465          79 :     if( !d->m_pj_crs || d->m_pjType != PJ_TYPE_COMPOUND_CRS )
    7466             :     {
    7467           0 :         d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
    7468           0 :         return OGRERR_NONE;
    7469             :     }
    7470             :     auto horizCRS =
    7471          79 :         proj_crs_get_sub_crs(d->getPROJContext(), d->m_pj_crs, 0);
    7472          79 :     if( !horizCRS )
    7473             :     {
    7474           0 :         d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
    7475           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    7476             :     }
    7477             : 
    7478          79 :     bool reuseExistingBoundCRS = false;
    7479          79 :     if( d->m_pj_bound_crs_target )
    7480             :     {
    7481           1 :         auto type = proj_get_type(d->m_pj_bound_crs_target);
    7482             :         reuseExistingBoundCRS =
    7483           1 :             type == PJ_TYPE_GEOCENTRIC_CRS ||
    7484           1 :             type == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_2D_CRS ||
    7485           1 :             type == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_3D_CRS;
    7486             :     }
    7487             : 
    7488          79 :     if( reuseExistingBoundCRS )
    7489             :     {
    7490             :         auto newBoundCRS =
    7491             :             proj_crs_create_bound_crs(
    7492             :                 d->getPROJContext(), horizCRS,
    7493           1 :                 d->m_pj_bound_crs_target, d->m_pj_bound_crs_co);
    7494           1 :         proj_destroy(horizCRS);
    7495           1 :         d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
    7496           1 :         d->setPjCRS(newBoundCRS);
    7497             :     }
    7498             :     else
    7499             :     {
    7500          78 :         d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
    7501          78 :         d->setPjCRS(horizCRS);
    7502             :     }
    7503             : 
    7504          79 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    7505             : }

    7510             : 
    7511             : /**
    7512             :  * \brief Check if coordinate system is compound.

    7664             : /************************************************************************/
    7665             : /*                            IsGeographic()                            */
    7666             : /************************************************************************/
    7667             : 
    7668             : /**
    7669             :  * \brief Check if geographic coordinate system.
    7670             :  *
    7671             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRIsGeographic().
    7672             :  *
    7673             :  * @return TRUE if this spatial reference is geographic ... that is the
    7674             :  * root is a GEOGCS node. Also if it is a CompoundCRS made of a
    7675             :  * GeographicCRS
    7676             :  */
    7677             : 
    7678       46281 : int OGRSpatialReference::IsGeographic() const
    7679             : 
    7680             : {
    7681       46281 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    7682       46281 :     d->demoteFromBoundCRS();
    7683       73064 :     bool isGeog = d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_2D_CRS ||
    7684       73064 :                        d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_3D_CRS;
    7685       46281 :     if( d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_COMPOUND_CRS )
    7686             :     {
    7687             :         auto horizCRS =
    7688          90 :             proj_crs_get_sub_crs(d->getPROJContext(), d->m_pj_crs, 0);
    7689          90 :         if( horizCRS )
    7690             :         {
    7691          90 :             auto horizCRSType = proj_get_type(horizCRS);
    7692          90 :             isGeog = horizCRSType == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_2D_CRS ||
    7693          90 :                      horizCRSType == PJ_TYPE_GEOGRAPHIC_3D_CRS;

    8037             :

    8040             :

    8042             : }

    8063             : /************************************************************************/
    8064             : /*                               IsSame()                               */

    8066             : 
    8067             : /**
    8068             :  * \brief Do these two spatial references describe the same system ?
    8069             :  *

    8080             : 
    8081             : 
    8082             : /**
    8083             :  * \brief Do these two spatial references describe the same system ?
    8084             :  *
    8085             :  * This also takes into account the data axis to CRS axis mapping by default
    8086             :  *
    8087             :  * @param poOtherSRS the SRS being compared to.
    8088             :  * @param papszOptions options. NULL or NULL terminated list of options.
    8089             :  * Currently supported options are:
    8090             :  * <ul>
    8091             :  * <li>IGNORE_DATA_AXIS_TO_SRS_AXIS_MAPPING=YES/NO. Defaults to NO</li>
    8092             :  * <li>CRITERION=STRICT/EQUIVALENT/EQUIVALENT_EXCEPT_AXIS_ORDER_GEOGCRS.
    8093             :  *     Defaults to EQUIVALENT_EXCEPT_AXIS_ORDER_GEOGCRS.</li>
    8094             :  * </ul>
    8095             :  *
    8096             :  * @return TRUE if equivalent or FALSE otherwise.
    8097             :  */
    8098             : 
    8099       23583 : int OGRSpatialReference::IsSame( const OGRSpatialReference * poOtherSRS,
    8100             :                                  const char* const * papszOptions ) const
    8101             : 
    8102             : {
    8103       23583 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    8104       23583 :     poOtherSRS->d->refreshProjObj();
    8105       23583 :     if( !d->m_pj_crs || !poOtherSRS->d->m_pj_crs )
    8106          21 :         return d->m_pj_crs == poOtherSRS->d->m_pj_crs;
    8107       23562 :     if( !CPLTestBool(CSLFetchNameValueDef(papszOptions,
    8108       23562 :                      "IGNORE_DATA_AXIS_TO_SRS_AXIS_MAPPING", "NO")) )
    8109             :     {
    8110       23474 :         if( d->m_axisMapping != poOtherSRS->d->m_axisMapping )
    8111        5465 :             return false;
    8112             :     }
    8113             : 
    8114       18097 :     bool reboundSelf = false;
    8115       18097 :     bool reboundOther = false;
    8116       18277 :     if( d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_BOUND_CRS &&
    8117         180 :         poOtherSRS->d->m_pjType != PJ_TYPE_BOUND_CRS )
    8118             :     {
    8119          38 :         d->demoteFromBoundCRS();
    8120          38 :         reboundSelf = true;
    8121             :     }
    8122       35976 :     else if( d->m_pjType != PJ_TYPE_BOUND_CRS &&
    8123       17917 :              poOtherSRS->d->m_pjType == PJ_TYPE_BOUND_CRS )
    8124             :     {
    8125          72 :         poOtherSRS->d->demoteFromBoundCRS();
    8126          72 :         reboundOther = true;
    8127             :     }
    8128             : 
    8129             :     PJ_COMPARISON_CRITERION criterion =
    8130       18097 :         PJ_COMP_EQUIVALENT_EXCEPT_AXIS_ORDER_GEOGCRS;
    8131             :     const char* pszCriterion = CSLFetchNameValueDef(
    8132       18097 :         papszOptions, "CRITERION", "EQUIVALENT_EXCEPT_AXIS_ORDER_GEOGCRS");
    8133       18097 :     if( EQUAL(pszCriterion, "STRICT") )
    8134           0 :         criterion = PJ_COMP_STRICT;
    8135       18097 :     else if( EQUAL(pszCriterion, "EQUIVALENT") )
    8136           2 :         criterion = PJ_COMP_EQUIVALENT;
    8137       18095 :     else if( !EQUAL(pszCriterion, "EQUIVALENT_EXCEPT_AXIS_ORDER_GEOGCRS") )
    8138             :     {
    8139             :         CPLError(CE_Warning, CPLE_NotSupported,
    8140           0 :                  "Unsupported value for CRITERION: %s", pszCriterion);
    8141             :     }
    8142       36194 :     int ret = proj_is_equivalent_to(d->m_pj_crs, poOtherSRS->d->m_pj_crs,
    8143       36194 :                                         criterion);

    8190             : /************************************************************************/
    8191             : /*                    convertToOtherProjection()                        */
    8192             : /************************************************************************/
    8193             : 
    8194             : /**
    8195             :  * \brief Convert to another equivalent projection
    8196             :  * 
    8197             :  * Currently implemented:
    8198             :  * <ul>

    8201             :  * <li>SRS_PT_LAMBERT_CONFORMAL_CONIC_1SP to SRS_PT_LAMBERT_CONFORMAL_CONIC_2SP</li>

    8291             : /************************************************************************/
    8292             : /*                    OSRConvertToOtherProjection()                     */

    8294             : 
    8295             : /**
    8296             :  * \brief Convert to another equivalent projection

    8302             :  * <li>SRS_PT_LAMBERT_CONFORMAL_CONIC_1SP to SRS_PT_LAMBERT_CONFORMAL_CONIC_2SP</li>
    8303             :  * <li>SRS_PT_LAMBERT_CONFORMAL_CONIC_2SP to SRS_PT_LAMBERT_CONFORMAL_CONIC_1SP</li>
    8304             :  * </ul>
    8305             :  *
    8306             :  * @param hSRS source SRS 
    8307             :  * @param pszTargetProjection target projection.
    8308             :  * @param papszOptions lists of options. None supported currently.

    8311             :  * @since GDAL 2.3
    8312             :  */
    8313          28 : OGRSpatialReferenceH OSRConvertToOtherProjection(

    8316             :                                     const char* const* papszOptions )
    8317             : {
    8318          28 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRConvertToOtherProjection", nullptr );

    8320             :         ToPointer(hSRS)->
    8321          28 :             convertToOtherProjection(pszTargetProjection, papszOptions));
    8322             : }
    8323             : 
    8324             : /************************************************************************/
    8325             : /*                           OSRFindMatches()                           */
    8326             : /************************************************************************/
    8327             : 
    8328             : /**
    8329             :  * \brief Try to identify a match between the passed SRS and a related SRS
    8330             :  * in a catalog (currently EPSG only)
    8331             :  *
    8332             :  * Matching may be partial, or may fail.
    8333             :  * Returned entries will be sorted by decreasing match confidence (first
    8334             :  * entry has the highest match confidence).
    8335             :  *
    8336             :  * The exact way matching is done may change in future versions.
    8337             :  *
    8338             :  * The current algorithm is:
    8339             :  * - try first AutoIdentifyEPSG(). If it succeeds, return the corresponding SRS
    8340             :  * - otherwise iterate over all SRS from the EPSG catalog (as found in GDAL
    8341             :  *   pcs.csv and gcs.csv files+esri_extra.wkt), and find those that match the
    8342             :  *   input SRS using the IsSame() function (ignoring TOWGS84 clauses)
    8343             :  * - if there is a single match using IsSame() or one of the matches has the
    8344             :  *   same SRS name, return it with 100% confidence
    8345             :  * - if a SRS has the same SRS name, but does not pass the IsSame() criteria,
    8346             :  *   return it with 50% confidence.
    8347             :  * - otherwise return all candidate SRS that pass the IsSame() criteria with a
    8348             :  *   90% confidence.
    8349             :  *
    8350             :  * A pre-built SRS cache in ~/.gdal/X.Y/srs_cache will be used if existing,
    8351             :  * otherwise it will be built at the first run of this function.
    8352             :  *
    8353             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::FindMatches().
    8354             :  *
    8355             :  * @param hSRS SRS to match
    8356             :  * @param papszOptions NULL terminated list of options or NULL
    8357             :  * @param pnEntries Output parameter. Number of values in the returned array.
    8358             :  * @param ppanMatchConfidence Output parameter (or NULL). *ppanMatchConfidence
    8359             :  * will be allocated to an array of *pnEntries whose values between 0 and 100
    8360             :  * indicate the confidence in the match. 100 is the highest confidence level.
    8361             :  * The array must be freed with CPLFree().
    8362             :  * 
    8363             :  * @return an array of SRS that match the passed SRS, or NULL. Must be freed with
    8364             :  * OSRFreeSRSArray()
    8365             :  *
    8366             :  * @since GDAL 2.3
    8367             :  */
    8368           8 : OGRSpatialReferenceH* OSRFindMatches( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS,
    8369             :                                       char** papszOptions,
    8370             :                                       int* pnEntries,
    8371             :                                       int** ppanMatchConfidence )
    8372             : {
    8373           8 :     if( pnEntries )
    8374           8 :         *pnEntries = 0;
    8375           8 :     if( ppanMatchConfidence )
    8376           8 :         *ppanMatchConfidence = nullptr;
    8377           8 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRFindMatches", nullptr );
    8378             : 
    8379           8 :     OGRSpatialReference* poSRS = ToPointer(hSRS);
    8380             :     return poSRS->FindMatches(papszOptions, pnEntries,
    8381           8 :                               ppanMatchConfidence);
    8382             : }
    8383             : 
    8384             : /************************************************************************/
    8385             : /*                           OSRFreeSRSArray()                          */
    8386             : /************************************************************************/
    8387             : 
    8388             : /**
    8389             :  * \brief Free return of OSRIdentifyMatches()
    8390             :  *
    8391             :  * @param pahSRS array of SRS (must be NULL terminated)
    8392             :  * @since GDAL 2.3
    8393             :  */
    8394          20 : void OSRFreeSRSArray(OGRSpatialReferenceH* pahSRS)
    8395             : {
    8396          20 :     if( pahSRS != nullptr )
    8397             :     {
    8398           7 :         for( int i = 0; pahSRS[i] != nullptr; ++i )
    8399             :         {
    8400           3 :             OSRRelease(pahSRS[i]);
    8401             :         }
    8402           4 :         CPLFree(pahSRS);
    8403             :     }
    8404          20 : }
    8405             : 
    8406             : /************************************************************************/
    8407             : /*                             SetTOWGS84()                             */
    8408             : /************************************************************************/
    8409             : 
    8410             : 
    8411             : /**
    8412             :  * \brief Set the Bursa-Wolf conversion to WGS84.
    8413             :  *
    8414             :  * This will create the TOWGS84 node as a child of the DATUM.  It will fail
    8415             :  * if there is no existing DATUM node. It will replace
    8416             :  * an existing TOWGS84 node if there is one.
    8417             :  *
    8418             :  * The parameters have the same meaning as EPSG transformation 9606
    8419             :  * (Position Vector 7-param. transformation).
    8420             :  *
    8421             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRSetTOWGS84().
    8422             :  *
    8423             :  * @param dfDX X child in meters.
    8424             :  * @param dfDY Y child in meters.
    8425             :  * @param dfDZ Z child in meters.
    8426             :  * @param dfEX X rotation in arc seconds (optional, defaults to zero).
    8427             :  * @param dfEY Y rotation in arc seconds (optional, defaults to zero).
    8428             :  * @param dfEZ Z rotation in arc seconds (optional, defaults to zero).
    8429             :  * @param dfPPM scaling factor (parts per million).
    8430             :  *
    8431             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success.
    8432             :  */

    8443             :     }
    8444             : 
    8445             :     // Remove existing BoundCRS

    8448         135 :             d->getPROJContext(), d->m_pj_crs);
    8449         135 :         if( !baseCRS )
    8450           0 :             return OGRERR_FAILURE;

    8593             : /************************************************************************/
    8594             : /*                             GetTOWGS84()                             */
    8595             : /************************************************************************/
    8596             : 
    8597             : /**
    8598             :  * \brief Fetch TOWGS84 parameters, if available.
    8599             :  *
    8600             :  * The parameters have the same meaning as EPSG transformation 9606
    8601             :  * (Position Vector 7-param. transformation).
    8602             :  *
    8603             :  * @param padfCoeff array into which up to 7 coefficients are placed.
    8604             :  * @param nCoeffCount size of padfCoeff - defaults to 7.
    8605             :  *
    8606             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success, or OGRERR_FAILURE if there is no
    8607             :  * TOWGS84 node available.
    8608             :  */
    8609             : 
    8610        4349 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::GetTOWGS84( double * padfCoeff,
    8611             :                                         int nCoeffCount ) const
    8612             : 
    8613             : {
    8614        4349 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    8615        4349 :     if( d->m_pjType != PJ_TYPE_BOUND_CRS )
    8616        4091 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    8617             : 
    8618         258 :     memset( padfCoeff, 0, sizeof(double) * nCoeffCount );
    8619             : 
    8620             :     auto transf =
    8621         258 :         proj_crs_get_coordoperation(d->getPROJContext(), d->m_pj_crs);
    8622             :     int success = proj_coordoperation_get_towgs84_values(
    8623         258 :         d->getPROJContext(), transf, padfCoeff, nCoeffCount, false);
    8624         258 :     proj_destroy(transf);
    8625             : 
    8626         258 :     return success ? OGRERR_NONE : OGRERR_FAILURE;
    8627             : }
    8628             : 
    8629             : /************************************************************************/
    8630             : /*                           OSRGetTOWGS84()                            */
    8631             : /************************************************************************/
    8632             : 
    8633             : /**
    8634             :  * \brief Fetch TOWGS84 parameters, if available.
    8635             :  *
    8636             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::GetTOWGS84().
    8637             :  */
    8638           5 : OGRErr OSRGetTOWGS84( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS,
    8639             :                       double * padfCoeff, int nCoeffCount )

    9166           0 :         return "OTHER";

    9171             : /************************************************************************/
    9172             : /*                              SetAxes()                               */

    9174             : 
    9175             : /**
    9176             :  * \brief Set the axes for a coordinate system.
    9177             :  *
    9178             :  * Set the names, and orientations of the axes for either a projected
    9179             :  * (PROJCS) or geographic (GEOGCS) coordinate system.
    9180             :  *
    9181             :  * This method is equivalent to the C function OSRSetAxes().
    9182             :  *
    9183             :  * @param pszTargetKey either "PROJCS" or "GEOGCS", must already exist in SRS.
    9184             :  * @param pszXAxisName name of first axis, normally "Long" or "Easting".
    9185             :  * @param eXAxisOrientation normally OAO_East.
    9186             :  * @param pszYAxisName name of second axis, normally "Lat" or "Northing".
    9187             :  * @param eYAxisOrientation normally OAO_North.
    9188             :  *
    9189             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success or an error code.
    9190             :  */
    9191             : 
    9192             : OGRErr
    9193           0 : OGRSpatialReference::SetAxes( const char *pszTargetKey,
    9194             :                               const char *pszXAxisName,
    9195             :                               OGRAxisOrientation eXAxisOrientation,
    9196             :                               const char *pszYAxisName,
    9197             :                               OGRAxisOrientation eYAxisOrientation )
    9198             : 
    9199             : {
    9200             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    9201             : /*      Find the target node.                                           */
    9202             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    9203           0 :     OGR_SRSNode *poNode = nullptr;
    9204             : 
    9205           0 :     if( pszTargetKey == nullptr )
    9206           0 :         poNode = GetRoot();
    9207             :     else
    9208           0 :         poNode = GetAttrNode( pszTargetKey );
    9209             : 
    9210           0 :     if( poNode == nullptr )
    9211           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    9212             : 
    9213             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    9214             : /*      Strip any existing AXIS children.                               */
    9215             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    9216           0 :     while( poNode->FindChild( "AXIS" ) >= 0 )
    9217           0 :         poNode->DestroyChild( poNode->FindChild( "AXIS" ) );
    9218             : 
    9219             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    9220             : /*      Insert desired axes                                             */
    9221             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    9222           0 :     OGR_SRSNode *poAxis = new OGR_SRSNode( "AXIS" );
    9223             : 
    9224           0 :     poAxis->AddChild( new OGR_SRSNode( pszXAxisName ) );
    9225           0 :     poAxis->AddChild( new OGR_SRSNode( OSRAxisEnumToName(eXAxisOrientation) ));
    9226             : 
    9227           0 :     poNode->AddChild( poAxis );
    9228             : 
    9229           0 :     poAxis = new OGR_SRSNode( "AXIS" );
    9230             : 
    9231           0 :     poAxis->AddChild( new OGR_SRSNode( pszYAxisName ) );
    9232           0 :     poAxis->AddChild( new OGR_SRSNode( OSRAxisEnumToName(eYAxisOrientation) ));
    9233             : 
    9234           0 :     poNode->AddChild( poAxis );
    9235             : 
    9236           0 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    9237             : }

    9289             : 
    9290             : /**

    9299             :  * definition will be assigned.

    9302             :  * OGRERR_UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION if MITAB library was not linked in.

    9310           5 :     if( *ppszResult != nullptr && strlen(*ppszResult) > 0 )
    9311           5 :         return OGRERR_NONE;
    9312             : 
    9313           0 :     return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    9314             : #else
    9315             :     CPLError( CE_Failure, CPLE_NotSupported,
    9316             :               "MITAB not available, CoordSys support disabled." );
    9317             : 
    9318             :     return OGRERR_UNSUPPORTED_OPERATION;
    9319             : #endif

    9368             :

    9378             : /************************************************************************/
    9379             : /*                        OSRCalcInvFlattening()                        */
    9380             : /************************************************************************/
    9381             : 
    9382             : /**
    9383             :  * \brief Compute inverse flattening from semi-major and semi-minor axis
    9384             :  *
    9385             :  * @param dfSemiMajor Semi-major axis length.
    9386             :  * @param dfSemiMinor Semi-minor axis length.
    9387             :  *
    9388             :  * @return inverse flattening, or 0 if both axis are equal.
    9389             :  * @since GDAL 2.0
    9390             :  */
    9391             : 
    9392        2485 : double OSRCalcInvFlattening( double dfSemiMajor, double dfSemiMinor )
    9393             : {
    9394        2485 :     if( fabs(dfSemiMajor-dfSemiMinor) < 1e-1 )
    9395           4 :         return 0;
    9396        2481 :     if( dfSemiMajor <= 0 || dfSemiMinor <= 0 || dfSemiMinor > dfSemiMajor )
    9397             :     {
    9398             :         CPLError(CE_Failure, CPLE_IllegalArg,
    9399           0 :                  "OSRCalcInvFlattening(): Wrong input values");
    9400           0 :         return 0;
    9401             :     }
    9402             : 
    9403        2481 :     return dfSemiMajor / (dfSemiMajor - dfSemiMinor);
    9404             : }
    9405             : 
    9406             : /************************************************************************/
    9407             : /*                        OSRCalcInvFlattening()                        */
    9408             : /************************************************************************/
    9409             : 
    9410             : /**
    9411             :  * \brief Compute semi-minor axis from semi-major axis and inverse flattening.
    9412             :  *
    9413             :  * @param dfSemiMajor Semi-major axis length.
    9414             :  * @param dfInvFlattening Inverse flattening or 0 for sphere.
    9415             :  *
    9416             :  * @return semi-minor axis
    9417             :  * @since GDAL 2.0
    9418             :  */
    9419             : 
    9420         347 : double OSRCalcSemiMinorFromInvFlattening( double dfSemiMajor, double dfInvFlattening )
    9421             : {
    9422         347 :     if( fabs(dfInvFlattening) < 0.000000000001 )
    9423          57 :         return dfSemiMajor;
    9424         290 :     if( dfSemiMajor <= 0.0 || dfInvFlattening <= 1.0 )
    9425             :     {
    9426             :         CPLError(CE_Failure, CPLE_IllegalArg,
    9427           0 :                  "OSRCalcSemiMinorFromInvFlattening(): Wrong input values");
    9428           0 :         return dfSemiMajor;
    9429             :     }
    9430             : 
    9431         290 :     return dfSemiMajor * (1.0 - 1.0/dfInvFlattening);
    9432             : }
    9433             :

    9458           3 :         poSRSWGS84->SetAxisMappingStrategy(OAMS_TRADITIONAL_GIS_ORDER);
    9459             :     }
    9460         420 :     return poSRSWGS84;
    9461             : }

    9468             : {
    9469         720 :     if( hMutex != nullptr )
    9470             :     {
    9471           2 :         poSRSWGS84->Release();
    9472           2 :         poSRSWGS84 = nullptr;
    9473           2 :         CPLDestroyMutex(hMutex);
    9474           2 :         hMutex = nullptr;
    9475             :     }

    9478             : /************************************************************************/
    9479             : /*                         OSRImportFromProj4()                         */

    9481             : /**

    9486         166 : OGRErr OSRImportFromProj4( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS, const char *pszProj4 )
