    9495             : /************************************************************************/
    9496             : /*                          importFromProj4()                           */
    9497             : /************************************************************************/
    9498             : 
    9499             : /**
    9500             :  * \brief Import PROJ coordinate string.
    9501             :  *
    9502             :  * The OGRSpatialReference is initialized from the passed PROJs style
    9503             :  * coordinate system string.
    9504             :  *
    9505             :  * Example:
    9506             :  *   pszProj4 = "+proj=utm +zone=11 +datum=WGS84"
    9507             :  *
    9508             :  * Some parameters, such as grids, recognized by PROJ may not be well
    9509             :  * understood and translated into the OGRSpatialReference model. It is possible
    9510             :  * to add the +wktext parameter which is a special keyword that OGR recognized
    9511             :  * as meaning "embed the entire PROJ string in the WKT and use it literally
    9512             :  * when converting back to PROJ format".
    9513             :  *
    9514             :  * For example:
    9515             :  * "+proj=nzmg +lat_0=-41 +lon_0=173 +x_0=2510000 +y_0=6023150 +ellps=intl
    9516             :  *  +units=m +nadgrids=nzgd2kgrid0005.gsb +wktext"
    9517             :  *
    9518             :  * will be translated as :
    9519             :  * \code
    9520             :  * PROJCS["unnamed",
    9521             :  *    GEOGCS["International 1909 (Hayford)",
    9522             :  *        DATUM["unknown",
    9523             :  *            SPHEROID["intl",6378388,297]],
    9524             :  *        PRIMEM["Greenwich",0],
    9525             :  *        UNIT["degree",0.0174532925199433]],
    9526             :  *    PROJECTION["New_Zealand_Map_Grid"],
    9527             :  *    PARAMETER["latitude_of_origin",-41],
    9528             :  *    PARAMETER["central_meridian",173],
    9529             :  *    PARAMETER["false_easting",2510000],
    9530             :  *    PARAMETER["false_northing",6023150],
    9531             :  *    UNIT["Meter",1],
    9532             :  *    EXTENSION["PROJ4","+proj=nzmg +lat_0=-41 +lon_0=173 +x_0=2510000
    9533             :  *               +y_0=6023150 +ellps=intl  +units=m +nadgrids=nzgd2kgrid0005.gsb +wktext"]]
    9534             :  * \endcode
    9535             :  *
    9536             :  * Special processing for 'etmerc': if +proj=etmerc is found
    9537             :  * in the passed string, the SRS built will use the WKT representation for a
    9538             :  * standard Transverse Mercator, but will aso include a PROJ4 EXTENSION node to
    9539             :  * preserve the etmerc projection method.
    9540             :  *
    9541             :  * For example:
    9542             :  * "+proj=etmerc +lat_0=0 +lon_0=9 +k=0.9996 +units=m +x_0=500000 +datum=WGS84"
    9543             :  *
    9544             :  * will be translated as :
    9545             :  * \code
    9546             :  * PROJCS["unnamed",
    9547             :  *     GEOGCS["WGS 84",
    9548             :  *         DATUM["WGS_1984",
    9549             :  *             SPHEROID["WGS 84",6378137,298.257223563,
    9550             :  *                 AUTHORITY["EPSG","7030"]],
    9551             :  *             TOWGS84[0,0,0,0,0,0,0],
    9552             :  *             AUTHORITY["EPSG","6326"]],
    9553             :  *         PRIMEM["Greenwich",0,
    9554             :  *             AUTHORITY["EPSG","8901"]],
    9555             :  *         UNIT["degree",0.0174532925199433,
    9556             :  *             AUTHORITY["EPSG","9108"]],
    9557             :  *         AUTHORITY["EPSG","4326"]],
    9558             :  *     PROJECTION["Transverse_Mercator"],
    9559             :  *     PARAMETER["latitude_of_origin",0],
    9560             :  *     PARAMETER["central_meridian",9],
    9561             :  *     PARAMETER["scale_factor",0.9996],
    9562             :  *     PARAMETER["false_easting",500000],
    9563             :  *     PARAMETER["false_northing",0],
    9564             :  *     UNIT["Meter",1],
    9565             :  *     EXTENSION["PROJ4","+proj=etmerc +lat_0=0 +lon_0=9 +k=0.9996 +units=m +x_0=500000 +datum=WGS84 +nodefs"]]
    9566             :  * \endcode
    9567             :  *
    9568             :  * It is also possible to import "+init=epsg:n" style definitions. Those are
    9569             :  * a legacy syntax that should be avoided in the future. In particular they will
    9570             :  * result in CRS objects whose axis order might not correspond to the official
    9571             :  * EPSG axis order.
    9572             :  *
    9573             :  * This method is the equivalent of the C function OSRImportFromProj4().
    9574             :  *
    9575             :  * @param pszProj4 the PROJ style string.
    9576             :  *
    9577             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success or OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA on failure.
    9578             :  */
    9579             : 
    9580         504 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromProj4( const char * pszProj4 )
    9581             : 
    9582             : {
    9583         504 :     if( strlen(pszProj4) >= 10000 )
    9584             :     {
    9585           0 :         CPLError(CE_Failure, CPLE_AppDefined, "Too long PROJ string");
    9586           0 :         return OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA;
    9587             :     }
    9588             : 
    9589             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    9590             : /*      Clear any existing definition.                                  */
    9591             : /* -------------------------------------------------------------------- */
    9592         504 :     Clear();
    9593             : 
    9594         504 :     CPLString osProj4(pszProj4);
    9595         504 :     if( osProj4.find("type=crs") == std::string::npos )
    9596             :     {
    9597         504 :         osProj4 += " +type=crs";
    9598             :     }
    9599             : 
    9600         506 :     if( osProj4.find("+init=epsg:") != std::string::npos &&
    9601           2 :         getenv("PROJ_USE_PROJ4_INIT_RULES") == nullptr )
    9602             :     {
    9603             :         static bool bHasWarned = false;
    9604           2 :         if( !bHasWarned )
    9605             :         {
    9606             :             CPLError(CE_Warning, CPLE_AppDefined,
    9607             :                      "+init=epsg:XXXX syntax is deprecated. It might return "
    9608           1 :                      "a CRS with a non-EPSG compliant axis order.");
    9609           1 :             bHasWarned = true;
    9610             :         }
    9611             :     }
    9612         504 :     proj_context_use_proj4_init_rules(d->getPROJContext(), true);
    9613         504 :     d->setPjCRS(proj_create(d->getPROJContext(), osProj4.c_str()));
    9614         504 :     proj_context_use_proj4_init_rules(d->getPROJContext(), false);
    9615         504 :     return d->m_pj_crs ? OGRERR_NONE : OGRERR_CORRUPT_DATA;
    9616             : }
    9617             : 
    9618             : /************************************************************************/
    9619             : /*                          OSRExportToProj4()                          */
    9620             : /************************************************************************/
    9621             : /**
    9622             :  * \brief Export coordinate system in PROJ format.
    9623             :  *
    9624             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::exportToProj4().
    9625             :  */
    9626         196 : OGRErr CPL_STDCALL OSRExportToProj4( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS,
    9627             :                                      char ** ppszReturn )
    9628             : 
    9629             : {
    9630         196 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRExportToProj4", OGRERR_FAILURE );
    9631             : 
    9632         196 :     *ppszReturn = nullptr;
    9633             : 
    9634         196 :     return OGRSpatialReference::FromHandle(hSRS)->exportToProj4( ppszReturn );
    9635             : }
    9636             : 
    9637             : /************************************************************************/
    9638             : /*                           exportToProj4()                            */
    9639             : /************************************************************************/
    9640             : 
    9641             : /**
    9642             :  * \brief Export coordinate system in PROJ format.
    9643             :  *
    9644             :  * Converts the loaded coordinate reference system into PROJ format
    9645             :  * to the extent possible.  The string returned in ppszProj4 should be
    9646             :  * deallocated by the caller with CPLFree() when no longer needed.
    9647             :  *
    9648             :  * LOCAL_CS coordinate systems are not translatable.  An empty string
    9649             :  * will be returned along with OGRERR_NONE.
    9650             :  *
    9651             :  * Special processing for Transverse Mercator:
    9652             :  * Starting with GDAL 3.0, if the OSR_USE_APPROX_TMERC configuration option is
    9653             :  * set to YES, the PROJ definition built from the SRS will use the +approx flag
    9654             :  * for the tmerc and utm projection methods, rather than the more accurate method.
    9655             :  *
    9656             :  * This method is the equivalent of the C function OSRExportToProj4().
    9657             :  *
    9658             :  * @param ppszProj4 pointer to which dynamically allocated PROJ definition
    9659             :  * will be assigned.
    9660             :  *
    9661             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success or an error code on failure.
    9662             :  */
    9663             : 
    9664        1879 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::exportToProj4( char ** ppszProj4 ) const
    9665             : 
    9666             : {
    9667             :     // In the past calling this method was thread-safe, even if we never
    9668             :     // guaranteed it. Now proj_as_proj_string() will cache the result internally,

    9722             : /************************************************************************/
    9723             : /*                            morphToESRI()                             */
    9724             : /************************************************************************/
    9725             : /**
    9726             :  * \brief Convert in place to ESRI WKT format.
    9727             :  *
    9728             :  * The value nodes of this coordinate system are modified in various manners
    9729             :  * more closely map onto the ESRI concept of WKT format.  This includes
    9730             :  * renaming a variety of projections and arguments, and stripping out
    9731             :  * nodes note recognised by ESRI (like AUTHORITY and AXIS).
    9732             :  *
    9733             :  * \note Since GDAL 3.0, this function has only user-visible effects at
    9734             :  * exportToWkt() time. It is recommended to use instead exportToWkt(char**, const char* const char*) const
    9735             :  * with options having FORMAT=WKT1_ESRI.
    9736             :  *
    9737             :  * This does the same as the C function OSRMorphToESRI().
    9738             :  *
    9739             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE unless something goes badly wrong.
    9740             :  * @deprecated
    9741             :  */
    9742             : 
    9743         407 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::morphToESRI()
    9744             : 
    9745             : {
    9746         407 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    9747         407 :     d->setMorphToESRI(true);
    9748             : 
    9749         407 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    9750             : }
    9751             : 
    9752             : /************************************************************************/
    9753             : /*                           OSRMorphToESRI()                           */
    9754             : /************************************************************************/
    9755             : 
    9756             : /**
    9757             :  * \brief Convert in place to ESRI WKT format.
    9758             :  *
    9759             :  * This function is the same as the C++ method
    9760             :  * OGRSpatialReference::morphToESRI().
    9761             :  */
    9762          65 : OGRErr OSRMorphToESRI( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS )
    9763             : 
    9764             : {
    9765          65 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRMorphToESRI", OGRERR_FAILURE );
    9766             : 
    9767          65 :     return OGRSpatialReference::FromHandle(hSRS)->morphToESRI();
    9768             : }
    9769             : 
    9770             : /************************************************************************/
    9771             : /*                           morphFromESRI()                            */
    9772             : /************************************************************************/
    9773             : 
    9774             : /**
    9775             :  * \brief Convert in place from ESRI WKT format.
    9776             :  *
    9777             :  * The value notes of this coordinate system are modified in various manners
    9778             :  * to adhere more closely to the WKT standard.  This mostly involves
    9779             :  * translating a variety of ESRI names for projections, arguments and
    9780             :  * datums to "standard" names, as defined by Adam Gawne-Cain's reference
    9781             :  * translation of EPSG to WKT for the CT specification.
    9782             :  *
    9783             :  * \note Since GDAL 3.0, this function is essentially a no-operation, since
    9784             :  * morphing from ESRI is automatically done by importFromWkt(). Its only
    9785             :  * effect is to undo the effect of a potential prior call to morphToESRI().
    9786             :  *
    9787             :  * This does the same as the C function OSRMorphFromESRI().
    9788             :  *
    9789             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE unless something goes badly wrong.
    9790             :  * @deprecated
    9791             :  */
    9792             : 
    9793         857 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::morphFromESRI()
    9794             : 
    9795             : {
    9796         857 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    9797         857 :     d->setMorphToESRI(false);
    9798             : 
    9799         857 :     return OGRERR_NONE;
    9800             : }
    9801             : 
    9802             : /************************************************************************/
    9803             : /*                          OSRMorphFromESRI()                          */

    9805             :

    9814             : {
    9815          24 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRMorphFromESRI", OGRERR_FAILURE );
    9816             :

    9818             : }

    9820             : /************************************************************************/
    9821             : /*                            FindMatches()                             */
    9822             : /************************************************************************/
    9823             : 
    9824             : /**
    9825             :  * \brief Try to identify a match between the passed SRS and a related SRS
    9826             :  * in a catalog (currently EPSG only)
    9827             :  *
    9828             :  * Matching may be partial, or may fail.
    9829             :  * Returned entries will be sorted by decreasing match confidence (first
    9830             :  * entry has the highest match confidence).
    9831             :  *
    9832             :  * The exact way matching is done may change in future versions.
    9833             :  * 
    9834             :  *  The current algorithm is:
    9835             :  * - try first AutoIdentifyEPSG(). If it succeeds, return the corresponding SRS
    9836             :  * - otherwise iterate over all SRS from the EPSG catalog (as found in GDAL
    9837             :  *   pcs.csv and gcs.csv files+esri_extra.wkt), and find those that match the
    9838             :  *   input SRS using the IsSame() function (ignoring TOWGS84 clauses)
    9839             :  * - if there is a single match using IsSame() or one of the matches has the
    9840             :  *   same SRS name, return it with 100% confidence
    9841             :  * - if a SRS has the same SRS name, but does not pass the IsSame() criteria,
    9842             :  *   return it with 50% confidence.
    9843             :  * - otherwise return all candidate SRS that pass the IsSame() criteria with a
    9844             :  *   90% confidence.
    9845             :  * 
    9846             :  * A pre-built SRS cache in ~/.gdal/X.Y/srs_cache will be used if existing,
    9847             :  * otherwise it will be built at the first run of this function.
    9848             :  *
    9849             :  * This method is the same as OSRFindMatches().
    9850             :  *
    9851             :  * @param papszOptions NULL terminated list of options or NULL
    9852             :  * @param pnEntries Output parameter. Number of values in the returned array.
    9853             :  * @param ppanMatchConfidence Output parameter (or NULL). *ppanMatchConfidence
    9854             :  * will be allocated to an array of *pnEntries whose values between 0 and 100
    9855             :  * indicate the confidence in the match. 100 is the highest confidence level.
    9856             :  * The array must be freed with CPLFree().
    9857             :  * 
    9858             :  * @return an array of SRS that match the passed SRS, or NULL. Must be freed with
    9859             :  * OSRFreeSRSArray()
    9860             :  *
    9861             :  * @since GDAL 2.3
    9862             :  */
    9863         617 : OGRSpatialReferenceH* OGRSpatialReference::FindMatches(
    9864             :                                           char** papszOptions,
    9865             :                                           int* pnEntries,
    9866             :                                           int** ppanMatchConfidence ) const
    9867             : {
    9868         617 :     CPL_IGNORE_RET_VAL(papszOptions);
    9869             : 
    9870         617 :     if( pnEntries )
    9871         617 :         *pnEntries = 0;
    9872         617 :     if( ppanMatchConfidence )
    9873         617 :         *ppanMatchConfidence = nullptr;
    9874             : 
    9875         617 :     d->refreshProjObj();
    9876         617 :     if( !d->m_pj_crs )
    9877           0 :         return nullptr;
    9878             : 
    9879         617 :     int* panConfidence = nullptr;
    9880             :     auto list = proj_identify(d->getPROJContext(),
    9881         617 :                                   d->m_pj_crs,
    9882             :                                   nullptr,
    9883             :                                   nullptr,
    9884         617 :                                   &panConfidence);
    9885         617 :     if( !list )
    9886           0 :         return nullptr;
    9887             : 
    9888         617 :     const int nMatches = proj_list_get_count(list);
    9889             : 
    9890         617 :     if( pnEntries )
    9891         617 :         *pnEntries = static_cast<int>(nMatches);
    9892             :     OGRSpatialReferenceH* pahRet =
    9893             :                 static_cast<OGRSpatialReferenceH*>(
    9894             :                         CPLCalloc(sizeof(OGRSpatialReferenceH),
    9895         617 :                                   nMatches + 1));
    9896         617 :     if( ppanMatchConfidence )
    9897             :     {
    9898             :         *ppanMatchConfidence = static_cast<int*>(
    9899         617 :                             CPLMalloc(sizeof(int) * (nMatches + 1)));
    9900             :     }
    9901        1237 :     for(int i=0; i<nMatches; i++)
    9902             :     {
    9903         620 :         PJ* obj = proj_list_get(d->getPROJContext(), list, i);
    9904         620 :         CPLAssert(obj);
    9905         620 :         OGRSpatialReference* poSRS = new OGRSpatialReference();
    9906         620 :         poSRS->d->setPjCRS(obj);
    9907         620 :         pahRet[i] = ToHandle(poSRS);
    9908         620 :         if( ppanMatchConfidence )
    9909         620 :             (*ppanMatchConfidence)[i] = panConfidence[i];
    9910             :     }
    9911         617 :     pahRet[ nMatches ] = nullptr;
    9912         617 :     proj_list_destroy(list);
    9913         617 :     proj_int_list_destroy(panConfidence);
    9914             : 
    9915         617 :     return pahRet;
    9916             : }

    9918             : /************************************************************************/
    9919             : /*                          importFromEPSGA()                           */
    9920             : /************************************************************************/
    9921             : 
    9922             : /**
    9923             :  * \brief Initialize SRS based on EPSG CRS code.
    9924             :  *
    9925             :  * This method will initialize the spatial reference based on the
    9926             :  * passed in EPSG CRS code.
    9927             :  *
    9928             :  * This method is similar to importFromEPSG() except that EPSG preferred axis
    9929             :  * ordering *will* be applied for geographic and projected coordinate systems.
    9930             :  * EPSG normally defines geographic coordinate systems to use lat/long, and also
    9931             :  * there are also a few projected coordinate systems that use northing/easting
    9932             :  * order contrary to typical GIS use).  See
    9933             :  * OGRSpatialReference::importFromEPSG() for more details on operation of this
    9934             :  * method.
    9935             :  *
    9936             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRImportFromEPSGA().
    9937             :  *
    9938             :  * @param nCode a CRS code.
    9939             :  *
    9940             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success, or an error code on failure.
    9941             :  */
    9942             : 
    9943       26245 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromEPSGA( int nCode )
    9944             : 
    9945             : {
    9946       26245 :     Clear();
    9947             :

    9949       26245 :     osCode.Printf("%d", nCode);
    9950             :     auto obj = proj_create_from_database(d->getPROJContext(),
    9951             :                                              "EPSG",
    9952             :                                              osCode.c_str(),
    9953             :                                              PJ_CATEGORY_CRS,
    9954             :                                              true,
    9955       26245 :                                              nullptr);
    9956       26245 :     if( !obj )
    9957             :     {
    9958          10 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
    9959             :     }
    9960             : 
    9961       26235 :     if( proj_is_deprecated(obj) ) {
    9962         824 :         auto list = proj_get_non_deprecated(d->getPROJContext(), obj);
    9963        1648 :         if( list && CPLTestBool(
    9964         824 :                 CPLGetConfigOption("OSR_USE_NON_DEPRECATED", "YES")) ) {
    9965         821 :             const auto count = proj_list_get_count(list);
    9966         821 :             if( count == 1 ) {
    9967             :                 auto nonDeprecated =
    9968         719 :                     proj_list_get(d->getPROJContext(), list, 0);
    9969         719 :                 if( nonDeprecated ) {
    9970         719 :                     proj_destroy(obj);

    9972             :                 }

    9993             : 
    9994             : /**
    9995             :  * \brief  Initialize SRS based on EPSG CRS code.

    9999             :

   10009             : /************************************************************************/
   10010             : /*                           importFromEPSG()                           */
   10011             : /************************************************************************/
   10012             : 
   10013             : /**
   10014             :  * \brief Initialize SRS based on EPSG GCS or PCS code.
   10015             :  *
   10016             :  * This method will initialize the spatial reference based on the
   10017             :  * passed in EPSG GCS or PCS code.  The coordinate system definitions
   10018             :  * are normally read from the EPSG derived support files such as
   10019             :  * pcs.csv, gcs.csv, pcs.override.csv, gcs.override.csv and falling
   10020             :  * back to search for a PROJ.4 epsg init file or a definition in epsg.wkt.
   10021             :  *
   10022             :  * These support files are normally searched for in /usr/local/share/gdal
   10023             :  * or in the directory identified by the GDAL_DATA configuration option.
   10024             :  * See CPLFindFile() for details.
   10025             :  *
   10026             :  * This method is relatively expensive, and generally involves quite a bit
   10027             :  * of text file scanning.  Reasonable efforts should be made to avoid calling
   10028             :  * it many times for the same coordinate system.
   10029             :  *
   10030             :  * This method is similar to importFromEPSGA() except that EPSG preferred
   10031             :  * axis ordering will *not* be applied for geographic coordinate systems.
   10032             :  * EPSG normally defines geographic coordinate systems to use lat/long
   10033             :  * contrary to typical GIS use). Since OGR 1.10.0, EPSG preferred
   10034             :  * axis ordering will also *not* be applied for projected coordinate systems
   10035             :  * that use northing/easting order.
   10036             :  *
   10037             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSRImportFromEPSG().
   10038             :  *
   10039             :  * @param nCode a GCS or PCS code from the horizontal coordinate system table.
   10040             :  *
   10041             :  * @return OGRERR_NONE on success, or an error code on failure.
   10042             :  */
   10043             : 
   10044       25444 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::importFromEPSG( int nCode )
   10045             : 
   10046             : {
   10047       25444 :     return importFromEPSGA( nCode );
   10048             : }
   10049             : 
   10050             : /************************************************************************/
   10051             : /*                         OSRImportFromEPSG()                          */
   10052             : /************************************************************************/
   10053             : 
   10054             : /**
   10055             :  * \brief  Initialize SRS based on EPSG GCS or PCS code.
   10056             :  *
   10057             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::importFromEPSG().
   10058             :  */
   10059             : 
   10060         332 : OGRErr CPL_STDCALL OSRImportFromEPSG( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS, int nCode )
   10061             : 
   10062             : {
   10063         332 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSRImportFromEPSG", OGRERR_FAILURE );
   10064             : 
   10065             :     return OGRSpatialReference::FromHandle(hSRS)->
   10066         332 :         importFromEPSG( nCode );
   10067             : }

   10069             : /************************************************************************/
   10070             : /*                        EPSGTreatsAsLatLong()                         */
   10071             : /************************************************************************/
   10072             : 
   10073             : /**
   10074             :  * \brief This method returns TRUE if EPSG feels this geographic coordinate
   10075             :  * system should be treated as having lat/long coordinate ordering.
   10076             :  *
   10077             :  * Currently this returns TRUE for all geographic coordinate systems
   10078             :  * with an EPSG code set, and axes set defining it as lat, long.
   10079             :  *
   10080             :  * \note Important change of behaviour since GDAL 3.0. In previous versions,
   10081             :  * geographic CRS imported with importFromEPSG() would cause this method to
   10082             :  * return FALSE on them, whereas now it returns TRUE, since importFromEPSG()
   10083             :  * is now equivalent to importFromEPSGA().
   10084             :  *
   10085             :  * FALSE will be returned for all coordinate systems that are not geographic,
   10086             :  * or that do not have an EPSG code set.
   10087             :  *
   10088             :  * This method is the same as the C function OSREPSGTreatsAsLatLong().
   10089             :  *
   10090             :  * @return TRUE or FALSE.
   10091             :  */
   10092             : 
   10093         314 : int OGRSpatialReference::EPSGTreatsAsLatLong() const
   10094             : 
   10095             : {
   10096         314 :     if( !IsGeographic() )
   10097         224 :         return FALSE;
   10098             : 
   10099          90 :     d->demoteFromBoundCRS();
   10100          90 :     const char* pszAuth = proj_get_id_auth_name(d->m_pj_crs, 0);
   10101          90 :     if( pszAuth == nullptr || !EQUAL(pszAuth, "EPSG") )
   10102             :     {
   10103           4 :         d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
   10104           4 :         return FALSE;
   10105             :     }
   10106             : 
   10107          86 :     bool ret = false;
   10108             :     auto cs = proj_crs_get_coordinate_system(d->getPROJContext(),
   10109          86 :                                                 d->m_pj_crs);
   10110          86 :     d->undoDemoteFromBoundCRS();
   10111             : 
   10112          86 :     if( cs )
   10113             :     {
   10114          86 :         const char* pszDirection = nullptr;
   10115          86 :         if( proj_cs_get_axis_info(
   10116             :             d->getPROJContext(), cs, 0, nullptr, nullptr, &pszDirection,
   10117          86 :             nullptr, nullptr, nullptr, nullptr) )
   10118             :         {
   10119          86 :             if( EQUAL(pszDirection, "north") )
   10120             :             {
   10121          86 :                 ret = true;
   10122             :             }
   10123             :         }
   10124             : 
   10125          86 :         proj_destroy(cs);
   10126             :     }
   10127             : 
   10128          86 :     return ret;
   10129             : }
   10130             : 
   10131             : /************************************************************************/
   10132             : /*                       OSREPSGTreatsAsLatLong()                       */
   10133             : /************************************************************************/
   10134             : 
   10135             : /**
   10136             :  * \brief This function returns TRUE if EPSG feels this geographic coordinate
   10137             :  * system should be treated as having lat/long coordinate ordering.
   10138             :  *
   10139             :  * This function is the same as OGRSpatialReference::OSREPSGTreatsAsLatLong().
   10140             :  */
   10141             : 
   10142           3 : int OSREPSGTreatsAsLatLong( OGRSpatialReferenceH hSRS )
   10143             : 
   10144             : {
   10145           3 :     VALIDATE_POINTER1( hSRS, "OSREPSGTreatsAsLatLong", OGRERR_FAILURE );
   10146             : 
   10147           3 :     return OGRSpatialReference::FromHandle(hSRS)->EPSGTreatsAsLatLong();
   10148             : }
   10149             : 
   10150             : /************************************************************************/
   10151             : /*                     EPSGTreatsAsNorthingEasting()                    */
   10152             : /************************************************************************/
   10153             : 
   10154             : /**
   10155             :  * \brief This method returns TRUE if EPSG feels this projected coordinate
   10156             :  * system should be treated as having northing/easting coordinate ordering.
   10157             :  *
   10158             :  * Currently this returns TRUE for all projected coordinate systems
   10159             :  * with an EPSG code set, and axes set defining it as northing, easting.
   10160             :  * 
   10161             :  * \note Important change of behaviour since GDAL 3.0. In previous versions,
   10162             :  * projected CRS with northing, easting axis order imported with
   10163             :  * importFromEPSG() would cause this method to
   10164             :  * return FALSE on them, whereas now it returns TRUE, since importFromEPSG()
   10165             :  * is now equivalent to importFromEPSGA().
   10166             :  *
   10167             :  * FALSE will be returned for all coordinate systems that are not projected,
   10168             :  * or that do not have an EPSG code set.
   10169             :  *
   10170             :  * This method is the same as the C function EPSGTreatsAsNorthingEasting().
   10171             :  *
   10172             :  * @return TRUE or FALSE.
   10173             :  *
   10174             :  * @since OGR 1.10.0

   10202             :     {

   10277           1 : OGRErr OGRSpatialReference::ImportFromESRIWisconsinWKT(

   10284           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
   10285             :

   10288             :     {
   10289           0 :         const PJ_TYPE type = PJ_TYPE_PROJECTED_CRS;

   10299           0 :                     Clear();

   10302           0 :                     return OGRERR_NONE;
   10303             :                 }
   10304             :             }
   10305           0 :             proj_list_destroy(list);
   10306             :         }
   10307           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;
   10308             :     }
   10309             : 
   10310           1 :     if( prjName == nullptr || unitsName == nullptr )

   10312           0 :         return OGRERR_FAILURE;

   10315           1 :     const PJ_TYPE type = PJ_TYPE_PROJECTED_CRS;

   10320             :     {

   10410             : /************************************************************************/
   10411             : /*                      GetAxisMappingStrategy()                        */
   10412             : /************************************************************************/
   10413             : 
   10414             : /** \brief Retun the data axis to CRS axis mapping strategy.
   10415             :  * 
   10416             :  * <ul>
   10417             :  * <li>OAMS_TRADITIONAL_GIS_ORDER means that for geographic CRS with
   10418             :  *     lat/long order, the data will still be long/lat ordered. Similarly for
   10419             :  *     a projected CRS with northing/easting order, the data will still be
   10420             :  *     easting/northing ordered.
   10421             :  * <li>OAMS_AUTHORITY_COMPLIANT means that the data axis will be identical to
   10422             :  *     the CRS axis.
   10423             :  * <li>OAMS_CUSTOM means that the data axis are customly defined with
   10424             :  *     SetDataAxisToSRSAxisMapping()
   10425             :  * </ul>
   10426             :  * @return the the data axis to CRS axis mapping strategy.
   10427             :  * @since GDAL 3.0
   10428             :  */
   10429           0 : OSRAxisMappingStrategy OGRSpatialReference::GetAxisMappingStrategy() const
   10430             : {
   10431           0 :     return d->m_axisMappingStrategy;
   10432             : }
   10433             :

   10621             : /************************************************************************/
   10622             : /*                     OSRGetCRSInfoListFromDatabase()                  */
   10623             : /************************************************************************/
   10624             : 
   10625             : /** \brief Enumerate CRS objects from the database.
   10626             :  *
   10627             :  * The returned object is an array of OSRCRSInfo* pointers, whose last
   10628             :  * entry is NULL. This array should be freed with OSRDestroyCRSInfoList()
   10629             :  *
   10630             :  * @param pszAuthName Authority name, used to restrict the search.
   10631             :  * Or NULL for all authorities.
   10632             :  * @param params Additional criteria. Must be set to NULL for now.
   10633             :  * @param pnOutResultCount Output parameter pointing to an integer to receive
   10634             :  * the size of the result list. Might be NULL
   10635             :  * @return an array of OSRCRSInfo* pointers to be freed with
   10636             :  * OSRDestroyCRSInfoList(), or NULL in case of error.
   10637             :  *
   10638             :  * @since GDAL 3.0
   10639             :  */
   10640           1 : OSRCRSInfo **OSRGetCRSInfoListFromDatabase(
   10641             :                                       const char *pszAuthName,
   10642             :                                       CPL_UNUSED const OSRCRSListParameters* params,
   10643             :                                       int *pnOutResultCount)
   10644             : {
   10645           1 :     int nResultCount = 0;
   10646             :     auto projList = proj_get_crs_info_list_from_database(OSRGetProjTLSContext(),
   10647             :                                          pszAuthName,
   10648             :                                          nullptr,
   10649           1 :                                          &nResultCount);
   10650           1 :     if( pnOutResultCount )
   10651           1 :         *pnOutResultCount = nResultCount;
   10652           1 :     if( !projList )
   10653             :     {
   10654           0 :         return nullptr;
   10655             :     }
   10656           1 :     auto res = new OSRCRSInfo*[nResultCount + 1];
   10657        6466 :     for( int i = 0; i < nResultCount; i++ )
   10658             :     {
   10659        6465 :         res[i] = new OSRCRSInfo;
